I've had this app on every phone I've owned since 2014 and I haven't had it freeze and glitch before. I'll be scrolling and the screen will start shaking, images/gifs/videos will start freaking out, and random letters in text will disappear before the app crashes and closes itself. Great app with a solid algorithm, and one of those rare cases where the ads don't interfere with your viewing experience— but this recent development spamming notifications needs to stop. I don't need to know every time a user I follow interacts with someone else. The filtering system could also use some work— posts with marked hashtags should be excluded entirely from search, not just have a warning attached to it.

The ads have gotten worse since last year, and are more aggressive. The worse ones are the ones that are flashy and overly animated, like the casino ones. I find them triggering as well. I'm uninstalling the app and using it through the browser instead. I liked the app, but I couldn't deal with the ads. Plus they slow down your phone.

The app refreshes every time I go out of it or put my phone to sleep for a minute. Any other app I can leave and return to the same spot I left off, but not Tumblr. so before the recent update if you were reading a post the end of the post was the end of the 'page' and you could select to click on a new recommend post from a cluster of images/posts. now however its just continuous scrolling which is terrible. not only can i not see how much longer i have to read something based on the scroll bar but i also cant choose the next post and have to read whatever order it generates. please put it back!

when I post or re-blog a post, the green pop-up is very useful. but for a while now, the pop-up is hidden under my phone's nav-bar. this is frustrating and messed up. more recently, when I click the back button while looking at something other than my feed, the app completely closes. (it doesn't quit) so I'm opening and reopening when I'm catching up with notifications. :/

Working okay so far, only one or two crashes. My main problem with this app is that it is lacking in comparison to the browser experience. So many basic functions are missing. "Well, that certainly didn't work. Please try again." - guys, that is NOT a helpful or even informative error message when a post fails. I love Tumblr, and I've used the mobile version for years. However, whenever I try to do pretty much anything in the app, it crashes. Even just leaving it open on my page makes it crash. Regardless, the mobile app is all but unusable now, so unfortunately I really don't feel I have another choice except to give one star. I've had tumblr on just about every phone I've owned since 2013, but the most recent update is just not up to par. This used to be my favorite social media app, but removing the reblog chain is such a bad move. It makes it impossible to find the source of a post, which is how I mainly find new blogs/people to follow. Also the change in the icon configuration/order at the bottom of posts (like/reblog/share) now makes it impossible to comfortably reblog or like posts while scrolling with one hand, I keep accidentally hitting the "share" button and it's very annoying.

I already saved my draft, but when I wanted to edit it again, the "save" button didn't save my draft, instead it posted it directly. As a result, my post was only half-finished :( Can you fix it? Also please revert the reblog/like/comment system to the old one (this is important to me). Before this problem is solved, I won't change my rating. Thank you.

Ads aren't necessarily a bad thing but they need to be managed better. I understand it's how tumblr keeps afloat but if every ad is gonna be borderline adult content or gore than thats an issue. The way ads appear is also very jarring, I think a more reddit approach should be taken; where you can easily see it's an ad but it also flows naturally with the scroll rather than breaking it. Other than that the only real glaring issue with tumblr is bots and app stability. Otherwise a good app.

Recent announcements to change how reblog chains work have concerned me to point that I cannot rate it well. To add, even if I wasn't concerned about how changes could destroy the app, navigating blogs was made more difficult (tapping a blog icon takes me to the most recent posts on the blog, tapping the top of a blog post doesn't take me to the post on the blog, just to a seperate instance thats just the post) annoying! Fix the old before adding anything new edit: the change made it worse :(

Have used and enjoyed tumblr for years, but the most recent update is an absolute disaster for basic usability. Short version there's no way to see all of the comment chains & replies associated with a particular post because they get split off with every new addition (removing the "social" part of "social media"). It also destroys a very important tool for preventing harassment, since all the users engaging in a harassing post can't be found (to block them) beyond the first link in the chain.

One of the best things about this app has always been the community aspect that comes from the chaos in notes. Ive found a lot of blogs I follow from looking through reblog chains, seeing cool additions, funny things people wrote in the tags, etc etc. They just killed this. It doesnt feel like the same app any more. It functions completely differently and feels so much more isolated. Really hope they undo this change so everything can feel alive again

Tumblr is the only social media I still occasionally scroll, but the app has gone downhill - every time I try to take a screenshot for a non-user or my personal collection, there is a pop-up that cannot be disabled. Better off to just stick to the browser version.

Data is inaccurate because of the lack of syncing, even when shown as connectedness. If you try disconnecting and reconnecting the applications, Google Fit will deny the connection, and there is no way to override. Google Fit is Junk!

I just want to say, so far, outstanding. I have seen Fitbit charge a crazy price for their "premium" services when it's mostly the same as this app! I can manage my Fitbit data through here with the fit to fit app, though that app still needs help. This app only needs the ability to sync data between third party apps possible! Add that, and possibly a mental health section as well, since you have meditation and whatnot, why don't we add an actual journal? Or a health journal? Just an idea.

It's a good app. I use a Fossil Gen 6 and while I use both apps Fit seems to work better. I like tracking my vitals along with my activity for the day. THAT SAID... I have a backlog of data and don't like weight entry set up like an "odometer" and the calendar is also tedious. Just let me type in the data please. Even better, you folks seem hard core on us talking to our phones, why not just me tell Fit, without typing/scrolling at all? And bring back desktop support like all your other apps!

It seems like it used to work, because I would get notifications about hitting my step target for the day. Now it's not tracking any activity at all, despite my settings being the same. Very confused. Nothing quite like getting to the end of a full and exhausting day on your feet and opening the app to see "0 steps today"!

I like the simple no nonsense interface that collates data from multiple apps and sensors without unnecessary bloat, gamification or ads. However, it's rather spoilt by random data loss and inconsistent syncing. It's annoying to watch your hard-earned heart points disappear in front of your eyes. Could be a great tool, but it feels like Google can't be bothered to sort out the long-standing bugs.

Third class app. Reduces km walked by end of the day by half. Never accurate. Forget accurate, it is nowhere close to mediocre. When it has to fetch old data, it crashes, restarts, dies again, restart, dies again. This is the wonder that Google makes. It never count the steps when I am walking and a phone call is also going on. Useless app.

Not syncing with my Samsung Galaxy watch for step counts... always seems to be about 1000 steps behind. All settings are set up for syncing... once in awhile the step counts match, most of the time, Google Fit is about 1000 steps behind. Only using it because it is the only thing that connects my watch to the Noom app. May just enter manually instead.

Why does it change my tracked activity run distance after 10+- minutes of saving? I finish my run and it correctly shows the map and distance (6.29 miles) but later the same map shows incorrect mile markers with a total distance of 7.09 miles. There's no logical reason that your app makes this erroneous change. It happens every time.

I really like this app when it's working. It's motivating to see how many heart points I've earned for my different workouts. However, it often fails to show the correct number of points. One day it'll show 20 points for a workout, and the next day those points will disappear. I have screenshots that show different number of heart points on the home page and on the journal page. It's very DEmotivating when this happens.

having serious issues getting the step counter to update, despite the source data showing that it clearly is receiving from connected apps. It's the only app that is able to connect to reward apps for step count but doesn't sync or update correctly. I've submitted feedback and tried all the troubleshooting but received no help

The functions are improving as it now tracks more info but it seems to be very inflexible and missing a lot of obvious features, such as tracking more types of exercise automatically and being able to activate it with Gemini or editing information for manual activity sessions. Even the reports and inflexible and leave a lot to be desired. With Google owning FitBit which has a much better app, I question why this app even exists.

this app, which used to be good, is now useless at tracking cycling routes and distances. I don't know why Google has disinvested in this and other location based trackers. this one only really works now in conjunction with other apps, which makes you wonder, what's the point. now you need to set a workout before you start and remember to stop it when you stop. same as other apps. the fact that you didn't need to do that with Google fit was the main usp, as well as automatically tracking walking

Negatives: 1 - as many people have pointed out, the app is kind of hit-and-miss with regards to accurately counting steps, and allocating heart points appropriately. 2 - There really should be a cumulative total distance walked available for each day, rather than having to add up each "walk" for a total. 3 - On MANY occasions, the cumulative number of miles DOES NOT ADD UP TO what the app told my on the particular day AS THE DAY WAS PROGRESSING! Positives: easy to read/navigate.

This app used to be really good, but it stopped syncing with other apps & the step counter is no longer accurate. Even when it counts steps, it will not total them correctly for the day. The email address listed under app support, is no longer active, so there is no way to contact anyone about this issue. Google really dropped the ball on this one.

I'm in a group mission and unfortunately it has a hard time connecting to post a distance or corresponding with the other app to record the activity. The other horrible issue is that it doesn't read your miles and steps accurately. I'm always shorted by a quarter of a mile, sometimes more.

I counted how many steps I took per minute and the step tracker for this app is accurate, it also matched another step tracker I used on a different phone BUT the calories are waaaay off! There is no way I walked slowly for an hour and burned 600 calories.

Actually I hate this app, for past 3 or so months it doesn't track your steps properly or it resets it all to together I have a Samsung watch that accurately counts all my steps everyday. some days the fit app works and sync with my watch most of others days it doesn't. Which I hate because I have a app that tracks my steps to earn rewards and I can't use it properly if it won't read my steps right. Because the app mainly uses Google fit and not Samsung health. Seriously please fix it

Since posting my last review of the app, I've changed the brand of bathroom scales that I use, but although the app they use does sync with Fit, only my weight is shared between the two apps, just like the last one did. None of the other measurements are. Again.

I set a daily goal of 55 Heart Points. I completed it and the app showed my goal as achieved. However, the next day, my previous day’s points dropped to 54 and the checkmark was removed. This is confusing. If the goal was not actually completed, why does the app show it as done at the time? It seems like the app recalculates activity later, but the goal status is not updated properly. This creates inconsistency and reduces trust in tracking. Please improve accuracy and ensure that goal comple

This app drops steps. I watched it go from 2.99 miles to 2.66 miles. Very discouraging. It has done this before. Unreliable. the only way that is reliable, or at least I hope it is, is to use the paced walking; then I feel like it counts all my steps.

Where are all these extra steps coming from? I have checked Garmin Connect and Health Connect, and they both reflect my steps correctly. Google Fit does not. I have turned off Activity Tracking and more as well, yet somehow, I have over 2000 MORE steps on Google Fit. Fix this once and for all; this app is not reliable at all. Health Points also constantly lower randomly.

I have been using this app for the last three years. Nice experience so far. One drawback I have noticed is that when I walk in a park etc it suddenly reduces total km walking while keeping the steps intact, but while walking on a straight long road the same step count gives more optimal km measurements.

love this app, simple but can do what I need. one issue though, when I'm pushing baby in a stroller and phone is inside the stroller, it registers the activity as cycling. that would be an extremely slow bike ride! then when I'm changing it to walking, all the stats stay the same, calories and heart points. That is surely wrong.

so far I'm enjoying the app, it's easy to use, tracks everything perfectly, and for those curious it works well with fitdoc. However one thing I wish it could do was tell you the amount of calories you're about to consume if you take a photo of the food you're about to eat by using the camera in the phone a nice alternative for those who don't have a smartwatch.

UPDATED April 2026: mystery update has no explanation? Used app for years, Grateful for its unique feature-Heart Points. It insures my heart rate is up - no matter what the workout or walks. So essential for all who have relied on this app for their health monitoring. Really hope they will "grandfather in" long time users and allow it to work as a standalone unsupported app on device, please. Not just any app - it's for our health & thus essential.

I would give this app a Zero. It doesn't count distance accurately compared to my wife's fitbit. The journal hasn't updated in 5 days. The map of my route looks like an abstract drawing. I checked my steps and distance halfway, when I finished my steps increased but the distance was less. Go figure The measurement of my pace is way off. I wish I could find an app that works without intrusive advertising.

was good and convenient with widgets so I could see how many steps I'd done through the day. but after the latest update it keeps deleting them. on the screen it was showing 9000 steps, then I'd tap to open and it will reduce them to 7000. then I exit out and it deletes more down to around 6000. it's also not counting all my calories burned after the latest update. I used to burn between 2500 and 3000, now it's only counting around 1800.

Not sure any thought has been given in designing charts. For example, considering 80/120 is considered normal blood pressure, I would expect the horizontal marker lines to be at every tens (e.g., 60, 70, 80, 90, 100, 110, 120, 130, 140, 150, 160) and not at some random (e.g. 75, 105, 135). A thoughtfully designed graph can help users whether they are in healthy range overtime (and if not, how far they are).

1★ – Very disappointing. The step counter keeps resetting during the day. It starts counting correctly in the morning, but suddenly all steps disappear and the counter restarts from 1. This defeats the whole purpose of a fitness tracking app because the daily activity data becomes completely inaccurate. I am using a OnePlus Nord and even after checking permissions and battery settings the issue still happens. Please fix this serious bug.

this app is garbage. The layout is terrible, the UI/UX is a mess. The functionality is bad as the app doesn't seem to register some workouts correctly. It deletes data, and doesn't calculate miles per steps correctly for things like treadmill work outs. Google can make world-class AI, but can't make a simple working fitness app?

it's not tracking my walking and it's affecting other apps that supposed to track my walking and earn points for it I'm connected to it's a problem. so like having problems with walk counter I've done everything and it still lagging and stops completely. like been walking for a hour counter saying around 1,000 like something is clearly wrong hear. it's affecting the other walking apps
Ease of use, good workouts, simple set up easy to follow and can be customized to different exercises for your preference

This app worked fairly well but recently it gives random numbers for heart points. I take a 3 mile hike every morning and get approximately 45 heart points for the hour long hike. Last week I got 168 heart points for the same workout and yesterday I got 256 points. I'm going to uninstall and reinstall to see if that fixes it. Edit...now this app doesn't track me very well. My hike is a big loop and on the map it will draw a straight line back to home from my farthest point. Come on Google!

Very buggy, main view doesn't show the calories, step and heart points, while those show via the widget. I use to sync my data from Notify for Mi Band app and I can only sync current month. Journal doesn't show anything while Sleep and Heart data are available on the main view. I get "Oops, something went wrong" errors all the time. I tried to clear the cache and data storage, still have the same issues. update, It now lost all my data for the past 4 years.

It works perfectly fine, it does what it's supposed to do. I don't understand why Google wants to "kill" it (soon). Health Connect is just a hub for data collection, it doesn't track anything or even have a user interface. Unless Google already planned to add more similar functions to Health Connect, "killing" Google Fit is just a very bold move!

Been using this app recently, good to keep track of my walks .. but just recently while recording, the app itself seems to stop tracking my process along the way, and need to start over again halfway throughout my process of walks. Have there been any issues arising on this similar case with anyone using this app.

Had to put a 1 star. It is not calculating all of my steps. I have a fitness watch that unfortunately isn't a big name brand (only one I can afford) so I have to manually enter my steps for the overall total. However, I am having a lot of issues adding more steps. The total is incorrect. Currently it seems to be adding them very slowly. My work has an app that connects to Google Fit to see our progress to enter contests. So it looks like I had much less steps than I actually did. Please fix!

I'd love to see a desktop or web version of Google Fit in the future. Having access to my fitness data on a larger screen would make it so much easier to review long-term trends, analyze detailed statistics, and plan my training — things that are harder to do comfortably on a phone.

Tracking, even for walking and with precision location allowed, is substandard. Even a straight road walk in loops is tracked with loop ends much short of the actual turn. In 9/10 times the distance measured is short by at least 20%. This renders the app useless for actual activity tracking.

I have a pixel watch 4. I use it to track workouts. These show up in the Fitbit app. But Google's Fit app is totally unaware. It shows me other Fitbit activities like walk, but not workouts. I share all data from Fitbit. Don't get it.

Very disappointed with Google Fit. I walk around 5,000–10,000 steps daily, but the app fails to track them properly. Many times, it doesn’t count steps at all or shows incorrect data. I’ve already checked permissions, battery settings, and updates—everything is in place, yet the issue continues. This makes the app unreliable and defeats its entire purpose. Please fix this ASAP. A fitness app that can’t track steps accurately is unacceptable.

Worked for me! But it takes more than 30 days to see result. You should keep a healthy diet too. I would've rated 5 stars if difficulty could be adjusted according to muscle groups. Some exercises are too difficult for an intermediate. Yes, you can adjust/choose exercises each day but one has to do it everyday to match their routine. Overall, good experience!

fantastic free resource for anybody looking to start their fitness journey. They also have some paid features if you would like the additional support. All in all, I think it's fantastic for people who are out of shape, looking to try an entry level way into fitness before hitting the gym, or maybe even a returning fitness enthusiast who wants to start things off slow. either way, I can't praise this app enough. only thing that they could improve on is maybe adding a few more stretches.

This app is amazing. No ads, doesn't ask for money and saves a lot of time. Most importantly, the exercises actually work unlike other apps. The level of accuracy is incredible. Great app for all level's. I'm 40 & overweight so was looking for something that would ease me in to getting in better shape, this app is great for that. I do a short 5-8 minute workout daily (minus the scheduled rest days). It's great to get the ball rolling & then over time I will push myself more, because doing too much when you're a beginner will only give you injuries. Would highly recommend!

I love this app. It has helped me greatly get some strength back, get into shape, and lose weight. Completely customizable to your abilities and preferences, and you can even swap out exercises you dont like for others. The ONLY negative I have is the notifications are kind of condescending. "are you gunna push through or are you gunna be another quitter?" I know they are meant as encouraging, but they aren't.
Truly well designed app. loved the overall experience and customization. Hoping to become a healthier version of mine. Cheers!

I’ve been using a weight loss app for some time now, and my experience has been mixed. The app is easy to use and provides good workout routines that can be done at home without any equipment. I like the daily reminders and progress tracking—it helps me stay a bit more disciplined. However, tracking calories every day feels tiring, and sometimes the app pushes too many premium features. Also, results are not instant—you really need consistency and patience. Overall, it’s a helpful all.

Even though it might not get you a major loss of weight in 30 days, it actually gets you something way better. It gives you a start of motivation to keep exercising and keep using the app. It starts a motivation inside you to keep doing what you're doing. For me it is really giving me the idea to stay disciplined through this app and never miss a workout.

I always loved to come to this app when I get in an energetic workout mood. I dont need equipment, just have to open the app and start. It's very easy to use. I absolutely love the new discover feature; I can work out in areas that I might want to focus on more. My only request is that I wish there was more leg/butt exercises. All in all an amazing app and would highly recommend 👌

Overall, it's a great app. But I can't find a way to switch to a different (intermediate) plan. The General Questions tab says to go to "My Training" but there's no such tab in the app. And I have tied everywhere else. I don't want to have to reset the entire app and lose my data.

This app makes you become a better person than you were a month ago because you can see the results and it all comes down to a calorie deficit and you have to stay committed when it comes to working on yourself. And it comes down to your fitness level from beginner to advanced. And all in all, it just takes time and effort.

easy to follow guide, have been using this for the past 2 years, although my weight doesn't change much, but i can easily fit 3inch less on my pants compare to before using this apps, the exercise also can be adjusted if you have an injury really recommend for home exercise👍👍

Really love this app, it pushes you towards your daily limit and encourages you to lose weight for real and I Love how it sets a specific time for specific exercises and also makes you choose the number of time you will do the certain exercise you are looking for.

App is perfect. It has positive effect, both physically and mentally . I would recommend it for anyone who is willing to spend half an hour daily for exercising. I think it's really unfair that I lost my 7 day streak just because I forgot to check in on a rest day. Could you please make it so that doesn't happen, or atleast so that people know to check in? Awesome! My first day using the app and I am already feeling the effect. I am sweating profusely! My only challenge is that the breaks in-between exercises are too short for me to catch my breath and continue with the next.

It's really a good workout app, I love the audio feature and and action being displayed. I would like to recommend the app to those who want to lose their weight. I like it and 5 minutes for the first day is reasonable.I also like how it gets easier to harder and it ends with a little calm and chill stretch at the end.

very good app and the AI guides us very good please if anyone want good body download this app for sure it will make a big difference in our body 1. one thing I want to tell that please add the diet chart in this please i will request you please and all is well thankyou please add a balanced diet chart in this please

it is a good app but overall I think it should be for kids all ages for kids 13 and up and also for kids that are self-conscious for their body th is would be a good app to lose a bunch of weight 🥵🥵👍

this app is so good and useful This is my first day but I literally can feel that I can lose my weight with the help of this app if I get any changes after completing my goal I'll rate this app 5
