very nice experience and app help to lose fat 😁. But some options are not there like height growth so I request you to update it 🙏🥺

it's the best app for weight losing first time when we learn it, it seems hard but when we reach to 30 days it's very easy and simple and it's helps my mind motivated. it also has serious injuries we need to stay balance in our weight If we are not in the wait and we are losing most of our weight we can have diabetes and I'm not making this app bad but it is good ♥️♥️♥️
This app has been crucial in my weight loss journey, I have trouble mapping out workout plans and this app really helped!

Mostly good, and far better than the popular alternatives. Two annoying problems. The Diary doesn't show the items in the accurate order anymore, which makes it hard to check if I entered them correctly. Also, if I open the app and search a food, about half the time, before I finish logging it, the app randomly takes me back to the main screen (with "Today" at the top) and I have to start again, which is frustrating and adds friction to the action that should be the easiest.

The new update to MyFitnessPal is not as user-friendly as the previous version. The one thing I like....the the food diary screen is the default. The log vs. +symbol... to make an entry is confusing. I know there is a learning curve, but the older version only needed a few tweaks, not an overhaul.

Used this for a decade...New UI changes are AWFUL and will finally force me to leave for another app. I wouldn't use this for free in its current state. Can't view the entire day anymore, everything is hidden in sub menus. Nutrient info for the day is three menus deep instead of easily accessed. Can't swipe back to previous days either, have to go through the calendar to a specific day. I'm out.

What happened to this app? It used to work so well. Now, it seems like half the app doesn't work anymore. Not even deleting and redownloading it, fixes anything. My progress bar is gone, the calculation for how much weight I'd lose is busted, if I click on my progress picture sometimes it just disappears and I lose it forever. It used to not be this bad. It's sad, because it's one of the best calorie counting apps. Plz fix things, these are all issues I've dealt with for a long time.

The latest app update introduced a new user interface which is not nice at all. It has added too many extra clicks to make food entries and exercise. Even the overall calorie summary overview lacks clarity and simplicity. Why did you need to complicate things when it was working so well? Earlier you could clearly see the calorie impacts from food and how much of that is mitigated by exercise and then compare the final with my daily target. This simplicity is gone now in the new interface.

For over a year I have not been able to save recipes I create. After entering all of my items, with a combination of scanning, searching the database or adding a new item...every single time the app gives an error. This is not a cheap app and this feature should work. A quick google or reddit search shows tons of people have this problem. Updated rating as recipes now work finally.

Great for tracking food, but the past few weeks hasn't synced between the app and the website, which means it doesnt update with any other apps synced. Tech teams advice: just update on the website. I dont have the time to sit down and double log all my meals, why not just fix a repeatedly raised issue?? Plus it constantly pushes for me to get premium despite all the issues.

ok I had started to use this app because it was recommended by health coach at work. A trainer at the gym i started going to recommended a different app. So for a bit I was using both to compare the 2 other app more user friendly, but I started a new program and they recommended this app. So i reinstalled it. I don't like having to pay to get the barcode reader, and I found that some of the macros don't calculate correctly when building a recipe. This is important when one is tracking them!

Full disclosure, this app has a significant glitch with respect to Android OS, whereby it does not sync with their web portal/cloud. This means that if you lost the data on your phone, or purchased a new phone, you would be SOL as none of your data would be able to sync because it does not exist anywhere else other than your phone! For me this is a big deal, but the developer is aware of it and is taking steps to remedy it. Other than this, I give this app a 4/5 (free version:)

Health Connect keeps duplicating or triplicating my workouts. I have Garmin connected to MFP and they keep bouncing workouts back and forth until it looks like I've burned thousands of calories that I haven't. I can't turn it off without disconnecting Garmin which I prefer to track my workouts. Fix it, please! Google Pixel 6

App is running worse & worse, day by day. This app used to be the best. I recently installed MFP because I started a serious weight loss goal. At first it was working perfectly. I'm familiar with MFP because I had used it 5 years ago to lose weight & it 100% helped me reach my goal then. Now, the app is VERY unreliable. It never picks up my steps from my fitness watch or other apps like Map My Fitness. It's adding exercises on days I've done zero (couch potato days). Very, very unreliable.

Overall a decent app, but there are a few annoying bugs. The main issue is that when I delete a food from my diary, sometimes it'll magically reappear a while later. When I add a food to my diary, sometimes it'll randomly add the food twice. The "add a recipe" tool is also broken. It won't let me manually input ingredients. I tried uninstalling and reinstalling the app, but that didn't help. Other than these issues, the app is a decent way to track your eating.

Update: that's a stupid to add to the notes This sucks that you can't track magnesium for your electrolytes. They don't even have the option to add magnesium when you create a food. Their help site says to create a food and add your supplements there. Hello!!! It's not listed. I mean how hard is it to add it to the list of nutrients. That post was a few years ago and it's still not an option. They have no problem changing the app for the worse. I have premium. I expect to be able to custom add.

Terrible, terrible design. User interface is the most confusing, non-intuitive design imaginable. Also, wildly inconsistent calorie counts for food items. I typed in 1 cup cooked quinoa and saw search results ranging from 120 calories to 225. The search results appeared crowd sourced, so accuracy is dubious. People just write in their opinions, defeats the purpose of counting calories. Stay away from this app.
although you have to pay for the app after a few sessions, it gives you time to test it out. love this app, gives me everything I need and some guidelines, easy to use and follow.

I had high hopes for this app, but sadly it is extremely limited. The food database is good, but the tracking is very clunky. The workouts are limited and have a fee for basic functionality. Actually, everything useful is locked behind a paywall and isn't done in a way that makes it appear worth the cost. The bones are there, but this just us not it.

UPDATE: I had to take this down to 3 stars now because as of how this app has become BOMBARDED with ads AND you can no longer scan barcodes without paying for the premium version of the app which also, by the way, removes the ads. How convenient! Otherwise it's a good app for tracking your food entries and viewing your nutritional intake. Those ads are very annoying though. If the cost wasn't so high I would consider purchasing just to remove them.

I've been using this app for years, and I have no problems with adding exercises to the diary until yesterday. Now when I click add exercise, the options for the type of exercise come up, but when I select any of them, the exercise window just disappears. Hard to track calories in and calories out if I can't input exercises. Please fix.

While I have been using the app since 2013, the latest update has ruined the functionality of the app. Previously, I could type a brand name into the search and pull up recent items. Now I have to type in a keyword from the item description. Bring back the ability to search by brand! It also deleted all my recent foods and I now have to re-enter them. An update should make the app better, not break previous functionality!

Absolutely horrendous app on Android. I can never use my previously logged food when logging something new in my diary unless I manually change a setting every time I log. as suggested on the website, I reinstalled the app to see if it fixes the issue, but it erased almost all my history. weird thing is that it didn't lose everything - just a few bits and pieces from every meal, which is even more odd. There is no easy way to contact support either and super disappointed with the user experience

Used this app for years to track my weight and food intake, I came back to it again recently to start being more serious about tracking my food again and they have taken away the scan bar code on the free version and the yearly subscription is a rip off just so I can scan bar codes. I'm going over to the Crono app instead as they have scan and are much cheaper. Sad that these apps are just turning into money grabbers all of a sudden when we are in a cost of living crisis.

Love my fitness pal! Been using it for years but unfortunately I cannot recommend this without the ability to barcode scan! Ridiculous this is part of the premium package that you have to pay for now! For someone like me who has ADHD, the ability to scan barcodes and add food quickly has been a huge plus. I don't have time to search and log food now. Gutted to be saying bye to this app! Hope this is revised in the future

No barcode scanner anymore as it wants you to pay for it. Doesn't have netball as an exercise, which is bizarre as it is a massive sport so that is inaccurate. Doesn't sync with any app whatsoever, again defeating the point. Will keep using it for a bit but tbh it's regressed since becoming popular and as an app will probably cease to exist in a couple of years.

This used to be a great app, but the most recent update is a major step back. Meals you save and then use on another day used to put the exact foods, so you could easily customize amounts. Now the meal gets added as a single item Search algorithm got a huge downgrade. You used to be able to search by maker and no longer can. Searching Starbucks in my recents doesn't bring up my Starbucks drinks, I now need to type the specific drink

Finally, I upgraded to Pro. 😆 I'm convinced with the help of this app that I will be back in shape by the end of this year. Lost 10 lbs thanks to tracking and adjusting. I wanted to try out the Intermitting fasting because of the many benefits. Picked the 16/8 time slot. Somehow, the eating hours are set to 4 hours, and I think this is an error, 🤔 as I can't set the eating window to 8 hrs. I'm not doing the 20/4 version 😅. That would be too hard for me. Please fix this ASAP. 😉 Thanksies 😊

Edited Rating to 1-star. App is pretty sub-par these days. Does not sync with my Fitbit app to transfer calories burned and steps. This doesn't work regardless of syncing is done via HealthConnect (updates sporadically and randomly) or directly with Fitbit. The entire platform is becoming useless if it cannot track the most basic and relevant metrics. Disappointing as a paying subscriber. Will cancel if it isn't resolved satisfactorily.

What Am I paying for, if every time I do a search for something I ate within the last day doesn't show up? This is annoying and time consuming. There really isn't much of an advantage to paying for a premium membership if I have to go through all the extra work to find the foods that I'm commonly eating anyway. Perhaps it's an algorithm change to get people to pay for the premium plus. Regardless, I likely will not be renewing my membership.

Truly this used to be a great app. Now upon reinstalling it I've come to find they've removed the barcode scanner for free users and you now have to pay. I refuse to pay for an app that does not work and the glitches this app has does not make me want to pay for it. The Fitbit steps sync up great for a few days and then they don't work anymore and no amount of uninstalling or reinstalling will change it. Customer service is so hard to get a hold off. Not worth my money and perhaps not my time

Generally an excellent app I've been using this app for nearly 10yrs and I've never really had any major issues. As with other reviews I agree that when searching for a product there can be too many choices with very varied nutritional info which can take time to find the one that matches the info on the tin, you might say. Biggest bug I have found is that the progress has changed and instead of my most recent weight being at the top the oldest one is displayed first. Hopefully this get fixed

all of a sudden the app has stopped retaining most, if not all of the foods I log. i log foods today and tomorrow there will be no history of it in my recently logged foods. this is so frustrating as I'm having to go back and find barcodes or manually search for every single food I am trying to log for the day. please fix this! this app used to be reliable, now it is giving me nothing but issues.

Would be Great if it Worked! Unfortunately, even in the Premium+ Subscription at its absolutely PREMIUM (EXPENSIVE!) price, does not work. The app consistently fails to save custom meal targets, defaulting to an even distribution. Website changes for these targets also do not transfer to the app. It does not retain links to third-party apps like Samsung Fitness, requiring daily re-linking for activity tracking. The app's calorie target remains inaccurately high compared to the web calculations.

I love the app, it helps me track my daily calories and helps me achieve my goals, however it seems to have an issue with the exercise tab. It wount let me build or create a routine, and the previously saved routines can no longer be accessed. The app freezes. Is this a bug with one ui 7? Will change the rating once it is fixed. Thanks

The lack of even email support is very frustrating. I pay for this app. It has a cool feature that shows macros right on my Android home screen. I can't see the Macros because MyFitnessPal says I have to pay for the app even though I've already paid. Bugs happen. No problem. Poor/non-existent support is unacceptable for a paid product.

MFP really helps with mindful eating. At the start, it takes some time to get used to tracking your intake, but the habit builds healthy intuitive eating. Love it and highly recommend! I also got the premium version, and the barcode scanner is such a game changer. Suggestion to Devs: Create the option to customize the nutrition the user wants to track. E.g. tracking magnesium/vitamin B etc.

I used to love this app. but nothing works on it. I have paid for premium for years. I still get ads. Now buttons aren't working and I can't log in even though I've updated my phone multiple times. Customer service is probably some bot cause there's no way an IT person is that dumb. I wrote a detailed issue email and it wrote back to try literally all the things I already said I tried. why bother. I'm about to just find a new app I'm so mad.

Seems solid, haven't used it much yet. The first thing that happened was that I changed my intake units for fluids to millilitres but on the watch it's still set to cups. Creating meals with specific items in it feels unintuitive, at least trying it out for 10 minutes without a tutorial... But hey, at least half of the app is ads

This is a great app as a free option to log and track calorie intake. It does feel like that is where it stops though. The app has potential with recipe listings, tips for weight loss, and meeting goals; however, there is no way to get a great experience with personalization unless you buy the premium version - which is way too costly for me personally. It is laid out nicely and very easy to search, log, and track your food. The app itself isn't overly busy or clunky.

Missing basic features It's a great idea to offer a database full of foods but when there is no option to correct nutritional values, it undermines the entire system. No one expects every food to be correct, that would be a neverending task, so where is the functionality to easily edit the data? e.g. fresh orange juice allegedly has 0% vitamin...

The log in problem has been fixed. I like the app and how it can track stuff even if you're not using the premium version. The only actual critique I can give is if only there was a way to implement measurements in grams for ALL food types. I like the different measurement options but I feel like to be precise and have some sort of credibility, there should always be an option to use grams (or ounces) as a measurement.

very glitchy app. I have trouble signing in. the dashboard is blank if I go to diary and then want to return to dashboard. It won't let me connect my fitbit app as it says an account is already connected when it isn't. I went on fitbit and disconnected every app connection it had. still won't allow me to connect. I won't even entertain paying for it while it is this glitchy.

Yesterday I could see all my logged food without having to expand each meal & easily see how I was doing against my goals for my chosen macros. Today that has vanished; all I see is how many grams total and the percentage split of my macros, without doing the maths manually (which kinda defeats the object) I can't now see how many more grams of protein (for example) I need to hit my goals. Looks like a downgrade rather than a useful update, I'm on a free premium+ trial and now unlikely to pay

This used to be more convenient. A lot of the great features that once were free and made using this app worth it is no longer free. I used to really like using this app to track but now its just extra time consuming and not very many features without paying $20 a month. Not worth it either way in my opinion. I used to use this all the time but has become a hassle without being able to scan the barcode to add it to my log.

Nice app to track calories. Lots of food in their database with complete information. However I have the premium version and some things are just lacking or not working well. Ex: We need a list of favorite foods. There are recent and frequent lists, but I have items that I know I use quite a bit and are in neither. I need a list of favorites i can search in. Also my steps dont sync well with Health Connect despite many refreshes everywhere, and I can't input steps for previous days manually...

Been really helpful to track my diet. However recently it doesn't seem to sync with Google fit very well. It's never been amazing, you need to make sure you visit Google fit app and MFP at the end of the day for it to sync properly but it was fine, now it doesn't seem to sync at all at the moment which is a shame

I've been using MFP on and off for over 8 years now. It's very easy to use, I love being able to see all nutrition (especially now that I have high cholesterol). I don't usually use the recipe finder feature but I appreciate it's available! 4 stars because I miss that you can't scan barcodes for free anymore.

2025 update: support doesn't respond. App is now deleting logged food at random. it's ridiculous that issues like this exist. Old review: My created meals keep getting deleted randomly. Often during or right after creating them. The whole app itself is full of clutter, i just want to track food. The scanner is pretty good.

Large database of items, but incredibly buggy. The update dialog is constantly bugging out, you can't close it and it covers everything. Closing and reopening the app doesn't solve it either. Random errors whenever you try to do things like update your goal as well.

I've been using MFP for a decade and with the exception of a few occasional bugs, it was easy to use and did what I needed, which was log my food and aggregate my nutrition. The new UI is so clunky and unintuitive. It takes a lot more clicks to log food and edit your food diary. At the end of the day when I'm trying to dial in my last snack, I have to click back and forth into and back out of the meal to see my total calories and macros. It's awful. And I'm very annoyed I just paid for a year.

This isn't just a calorie counter, it's the way to track & balance an eating plan. When I can see what nutrients I eat, I'm motivated to eat the right foods...not empty calories. I've been using this app for almost 5 months, with great results for my health! 5/26 review: I updated the app & I'm quite disappointed. In the old version, it was easy to see everything at a glance -- logged foods, goal percentages, etc. Now everything is harder to find. Less user friendly. Down from 5 stars to 2.

I don't usually leave reviews, but I would like to state this update absolutely bugged my calories (UI prior was fine too). It was perfectly fine this morning and now every day I've logged has 5,000 calories in surplus. I've tried reinstalling but that didn't work-- it's permanently logged multiple entries that weren't there before. I understand I'm one frustrated out of many, many long-term users who may be fine with this, but I'm not. I'm moving onwards; good luck, MFP.

Despite the fact that scanning disappeared from the free version, I kept the app because I liked the layout better than others. However, the latest update is horrendous. I want to see the entire list of food that I've eaten for a day. I don't want to click on a meal to see it by scrolling through like I used to be able to do. I also liked having the exercise list at the bottom of the food diary. The layout is no longer intuitive. I believe I'll be switching to a different app.

Just want to point out one issue: Fitbit does not track average sleep time accurately, specifically when you miss a night of sleep due to being up all night. I work in highway maintenance and have seen my averages remain much higher than they should when I've been up all night plowing. I submitted feedback in the app but wanted to bring attention to it here as well.

I recently started using the Stress and Mindfulness Feature on the app. While it's good at first your options are limited, unless of course you have Fitbit premium which you have to pay for. At the start of the week you have access to the library which you gives you a fair bit of free options. But after that, the rest of the week only 'Beginning Your Journey' is available with only 6 'Available to You' sessions. It gets a little repetitive, and you can't manually add a session.

It's a mess. The sync doesn't work, when it does it doesn't sync everything. Not a single number matches between my phone and my pixel watch, after a workout it syncs "strength training" but not the separate kickboxing session I do right after, however it seems to be taking the calories into account. What is this? How is it that for every good google product there are 5 bad ones, this is one of the bad ones. The other features are "ok" but not spectacular, the training videos are a joke.

Meh, I could do without all the email ads telling me to buy a new one. I just want the app to talk to the device like it is supposed to. It does, most of the time...things like pushing a grocery cart or walking on a treadmill the device and app don't agree on what's happening. So the app doesn't record anything. Edit...downgraded to 2 stars still. now the battery and phone won't sync at all. customer service is even worse than before.

I used to love my Fitbit but I'll be replacing it. Fitness score is inaccurate, and sends messages telling you to increase or decrease cardio load but there is no logic to it and even if you switch cardio load off, the messages still come. Now, it stops recording sleep in the middle of the night and messages you to tell you your sleep is poor. It won't let you edit it to reflect your actual sleep, and you can't stop it messaging. I've contacted support on all these issues and nothing is fixed.

I have been using this app for many years now and when it works it works great! the new public view is interesting and great for coming up with workouts etc, one thing is I wish it would put nutrition in the today page like the old version, but as of the last day or so it has not been tracking my calorie burn properly as the old version currently says I have burned 500 less then I have and the new one is almost accurate but not quiet, this makes tracking my deficit difficult

Consistently loses connection, will not send text notifications to the device and have to uninstall/reinstall at least once a month. Haven't even had this fitbit a year and I'm ready to go back to Samsung watch. I've stuck with fitbit for 7 years, transitioned to Samsung for a year and went back. Time to go as the Google move has been a pain.

Absolute waste of time and money. Charge 5 and app worked just fine until being forced to merge to a google account. Now the watch dies randomly, even when it had a 100% charge a few hours prior. And the app won't open more than half the time, just get a black screen. Worthless disaster of a company. Never purchasing fitbit products ever again. Edit: Google's response to this was even more useless than the app and watch.

why did cardio fitness score suddenly stop working after 12 Feb 2026 ? before this date, I got told I was within a range of scores. Then on 12 Feb I was given a single number (rather than a range) - and since then (5 days later) there has been no score at all. The change in behavior just prior to total loss of functionality indicates it is related to a 'bad' coding change. I have the latest version of the app (released in early Feb) but I do not know when the update was loaded on my phone.
Good App, easy to use, full of tips and guidance to help in doing several gym exercises.
This is a great app. I started using it during Covid time. The exercises helps loose belly fat.

New-ish issue. When tracking my calorie intake, the food section does not update with my calories out and just says I'm over budget (0 expended), even though I'm not. I've tried force stopping the app and clearing the cache to no avail. Could we add a refresh button here or just fix the auto update functionality? When it works, the calorie tracking is much better than other apps I've tried. (Note I'm on Android) Thanks!

I just discovered that Fitbit has been counting my drives as steps taken. I drive 4 hours a day and the first 2 hours it's already counted 1000 steps, meaning my reliance on the tracker for my steps goal has been highly inaccurate and sabotaging my goals.. the workaround does not exist anymore, driving is not an exercise I can find and log on the app. this has been an issue for 10+ years. insane.

Every the app just stops working altogether and everytime I open it its nothing but a blank white screen. The only way I've found to fix this is to uninstall the app and reinstall and re-sign in every time then redo all the settings and reconnect to health connect and Bluetooth then reset up notification settingsfor the watch. It is VERY annoying especially when I don't ever have to time to do all that when all I want to do is log or look at my calories or steps.

I've been using this for several years now and it's mostly okay, though it sometimes refuses to sync and I have to restart both my phone and my watch to get it to work (which is annoying when I'm missing text messages) and now lately every few days, the app just loads a blank screen and no amount of force closing the app works, I have to restart my phone to open the app.

app keeps crashing. I've tried to force stop and restart countless times. Every day, it crashes on me though. I'm fed up. I used to love fitbit, but it's gone downhill. Looking at past reviews, they have been aware for a year. Nothing has changed, seems they don't care about customers. I'll be looking into another fitness watch now.

Generally happy with the app. On a day to day basis, I can get into the app, see the information I want quickly, and then get out again. The charts are helpful and flexible as well, letting me define my motivation. I'm less enthused by my of the hardware problems I've experienced, but that shouldn't reflect poorly on the app.

I've used Fitbit for a few years now. I have premium, for a while it didn't even feel worth it to keep premium due to how little it offered that I used. The most recent updates and the addition of the AI coach has 100% pulled me back in. I'm on it a few times a day and it feels so much more motivating and like a positive experience. I wish the new coach helped with food/calorie tracking though instead of flipping between app versions.

I loved my fitbit Versa - it always synced and never had an issue all the years I had it. The face came apart and it was done. Update: I now have a Sense 2. I'll never buy one again. I CONSTANTLY have to reinstall this app at least once a week. It gives me a white screen. Cleared cache. reinstall. same. Don't by a fitbit. I'm buying a Samsung watch. 4/21/26 Update: I'm still having to reinstall once a week, sometimes more. As soon as my fitbit dies I'll be changing brands. So disappointing.

Until the recent update, I was very happy with all aspects of the app. Now though, the weight will not show as stones/pounds, which is my preference. I've tried the suggestions given in response to other complaints about the weight settings, but to no avail. It will switch to kg and show kg but when stone is selected, it shows as pounds. Very frustrated by the change and non-fix.

Use to be a great app. Lots of data immediately available in easy to read format. Every update removes more of that. Words replaced with cryptic icons. Graphics so large you have to scroll and scroll to see stuff. Most data that was available on the homescreen has been buried in nested menu clicks. Tips filling the screen that you can't disable. Awful. As soon as I find a replacement with a decent food budget, this app is uninstalled. Only good thing left in the app. Period. Don't buy a Fitbit.

Update: you would think with many of us having the same issue with sync that we'd get a better response than the basic script or an actual fix. I have followed ALL of the trouble shooting tips. * Don't love it anymore. Keeps saying "couldn't sync to charge 5" although it's syncing steps zone and calories. I've followed every help tip and resource under the help section and nothing has worked. Idk what yall did in the update but it's not been good.

A very helpful app but with a few problems. It appears that I must rephrase my problems as the developer is telling me to do things that I already have done, so here we go. I cannot choose 'dancing' on my fitbit as a workout, so attempt to change the workout that app assigns automatically from 'aerobic' to 'dancing', which it does. It also changes it back to 'aerobic' as soon as I exit the edit facility. This is highly frustrating!

Same problem as reported on many sites. I added a spin. At first it wouldn't save at all then I called it another exercise it saved that exercise. I went back in and changed the name to spin and resaved it. Now it will not save the calories amount that I'm manually entering from another spin class. This app has gone downhill. I hope they fix it soon. All of this "we are sorry to hear about your issue" isn't helping solve the problem. We need action!!

Food logging is garbage. Especially the "recent" tab, I accidentally logged something I've never actually eaten before about a year ago. Immediately deleted the entry and never logged it again. It has been stuck at the top of my recent tab for months. The list also contains various other things I haven't eaten in years. Meanwhile, things I've logged every day for weeks aren't on there at all or are at the very bottom.

I've been using Fitbit for years now. The past year has made me want to give it up. The biggest issue is not saving my tracked exercises. If I go on a 30 minute run for example, it doesn't get saved in the app. It's very frustrating when trying to go back and look at my stats to compare runs or any exercise. And it's been like this for several updates.
The best fitness app in the world If it had a ten out of five stars I would give it a ten

I was tracking my calories in My Fitness Pal, which is supposedly integrated with Fitbit. It isn't. Don't even bother. It was a major hassle. But the calorie tracker in Fitbit is close enough, so I just use that. Sometimes the tracker just stops in the middle of a workout, with no warning, so there is no record, and that sucks. Sometimes the device won't sync to the app. It's not a seamless experience. :/

Yesterday while reviewing my year-to-date average sleep, I refreshed and my number dropped significantly. I've tried various things, but my numbers are still incorrect. I can't find any information on how I might be able to correct this. Google seems to usually make things easier, but they've taken over this Fitbit app and removed the fun (removed the group challenges) AND made it much less practical to use (screen display). After 12 years, Im looking into other fitness tracker options. :-(

Today the app has stopped importing dietary data from MFP. So now it looks as though I'm burning calories but not eating. Why do you have to muck around with an app that works perfectly well so that now it doesn't.

Since Google bought out Fitbit, I have had nothing but problems with my device staying synced. It will randomly connect and disconnect. I miss text messages and phone call because it won't stay connected. I won't even get started on the issues with trying to change devices. I'm not sure if they are trying to phase out the older models (Versa 2) and make you buy a newer one or if they just don't know what they're doing. Either way it's very irritating not having the functionality you paid for.

for an app with a premium service the support is a complete joke. try and report a bug and they'll first need constant proof you're not just playing a prank and then make up standard script BS about restarting your phone and toggling a setting for step counting when the issue has nothing to do with that. Spend the money elsewhere.

Didn't care for last updates, but recent updates even worse! I can no longer track jogging in place as part of my routine as I need to stick to indoor workouts due to decline in neighborhood. If I do a brisk walk in place it detects elliptical. I wish I could remove that option a together, but when I remove it on app it doesn't translate to my device. Horrible changes... does not work for me at all.

This is my third Fitbit. I really like it because it's small. It's convenient, and it was rather easy to use... But they never last very long. I can't keep this one synced. It won't send notifications or text messages and I agree with all of the ones that say the help articles don't work. I've un-installed the app. I've turned off the Bluetooth. I've done everything it says to do, and it still doesn't work.

I've been a Fitbit user since 2019 and won't use them when my device inevitably bricks itself as soon as it is out of warranty. I use this to track sleep and activity and have had so many issues with the device refusing to sync. Enough that I have lost a significant amount of data (more days not tracked than tracked last month). The service is at rock bottom. I'm getting tired of being told my issues are because I am doing something wrong when the issue is always on your end.

You can tell when there's an app update because your fitbit struggles to sync and won't show any notifications from your phone. I've been a fitbit user since 2015 and it's been inconsistent lately. Not sure if it's because of Google buy-out or what. But, frustrated that things aren't working properly because when they do, it's awesome!

that's it. after cancelling my premium a month ago today made the decision to purchase a Garmin. Google messed up a wonderful app and devices. After 9 years I have to switch. Biggest issues broken sync phone app. I have to reinstall the app multiple times. I clear data in the phone and set up the app regularly (daily sometimes more). It is a hassle to use the Fitbit app for the last 3 months or so.

After years of working wonderfully, now suddenly it won't track my sleep properly. I was using it to determine how well my sleep meds were working, as it would show when I was restless and awake. Well now it doesn't show any of that data. It just shows the time I went to sleep and the time I wake up, and it's very inaccurate. It's now a glorified pedometer and that's about it... (and it barely updates my steps either).

redoing my review from 3 to 1 stars. I find it hilarious that google has the audacity to want to charge you $80 a year for premium on an app that doesn't even work 70% of the time. My phone will not sync to my watch and I have to restart it daily to get it the app to sync, I've had to completely delete the app and reinstall it many times to get the Nutrition section to recognize calories out, and many more things thar just makes this app more of a hassle than anything else. Do better Google.

Since Google and the latest updates, everything is broken. The new period tracker is awful and inaccurate. Sleep is constantly logging while awake. Can't edit exercises, so I guess I did 3 hours of yoga and slept 12 hours. It has been a while, and nothing is fixed. Clearly, they don't care. After a decade, I'm moving to something else.

Since literally the day my account switched to Google account login from the Fitbit login, this app will not show me my step data. I'll sync it and it might show it to me briefly, but at midnight it's gone. It's ridiculous that it's been 6 months and the problem still persists. Weirdly my weekly roundup email will show my steps, and the app work uses for the wellness program receives it, so the data clearly lives somewhere. Just this stupid app can't seem to figure it out how to show it to me!

This app has a pretty clunky interface, and now less than 2 months into using my Fitbit, it refuses to sync. The only thing that forces a sync is removing the device entirely from the app, but this discards my sleep data, which is the only reason I purchased this thing. I've tried all the fixes I've seen online because this issue has been around for almost a decade, and nothing else works. My phone sees the device in the Bluetooth menu, so the issue is with this unoptomized app
many exercises, good instructions, but slightly unintuitive interface. would also be nice to add homescreen widgets

The weight management section has been ruined. I set it to stone but I have to enter in pounds meaning I have to convert every time. The graphs are less detailed and are now pretty useless. I find the whole interface totally confusing and mixed up. The design changes are not an improvement. I see another review with similar complaints. She was told to log out, reboot her phone and log in again. I can't even see how to log out to follow those instructions. Interfaces should be more intuitive!

Another update, another loss of functioning that is going to make me look for another platform. My Fitbit will no longer sync with the app and none of the steps, which used to work work now. On top of that, it's not even letting me enter data manually into the app. I updated thinking that would help and no such luck. The Google takeover was a mistake.

I have had a few ongoing issues where my logged run shows on my watch and the home screen 1.8 miles and then when I click into the run it says that it was 1.9 miles. Also, ever since the crash the other day my calories burned are no longer are showing within the food section (always shows 0). I have restarted, installed updates, force closed, cleared cache everything I have been told and still nothing. Not worth a premium membership if you can't even keep the free parts working.

The issue I seem to be having lately is when trying to open the app I'm met with a blank screen. No amount of closing down, restarting the app, restarting my phone or even uninstalling and reinstalling the app fixes it. it usually lasts at least 24-48hours and when it finally decides to function again, my data for that time period is lost. For someone who is trying to track their consistency, this is super frustrating!

Missing some pretty basic functionality. I was hoping for a one stop fitness data hub. Unfortunately it doesn't import weight biometrics or nutrition data from Health Connect. The data is there, my other apps share it. So far Google fit is still the best at having the most complete summary. I do like how Fitbit presents the data it does handle, but I'm absolutely not paying for premium insights if they can't even import basic data.

I've been using fitbits for years, and premium briefly. When they updated the app and changed the period tracker, it was a step backwards - I actually used it to plan far ahead for holidays (no one wants a beach holiday with aunt flo). Many other aspects of the app lost detail, inc. the sleep tracker was no longer intuitive. The various training videos were user friendly. However, learning how to use the app again post update was not on my to do list.

Switching from Fitbit and tossing mine. Fun fact - it doesn't actually need your location information to sync. Rather, they made it necessary specifically so that they can - per their privacy policy - selI your location information. They will claim they need that permission to sync data - as a developer, I can assure you this is untrue. They also cleverly made it so that in order to do basic things, like setting alarms, you have to use the app, then sync... And thus give them location access. 👎

Edit: useless reply from dev, I'd already tried those. Tried every troubleshooting step but this app is broken beyond repair now. Coach (Premium) throws errors & never worked at all. Years worth of exercise data disappeared overnight. UI won't stop changing & it's still worse than the previous intuitive, colourful one. Sync not working more often than it does. Shocked at how it went from a decent product to a useless piece of hardware overnight. Time for an upgrade, never Fitbit again.

Recent version has broken capturing Run events in regards to the distance of the run. It doesn't show it at all upon sync with device. Even when I edit the event and add the distance, and save it... It doesn't persist, in fact returning to the main screen shows the distance goes a brief moment and replaced it with calories within a second. Very basic attribute of a run not being captured or stored upon edit is really regressive.

I have not used this app in about 2 years. I recently decided I wanted to lose some weight and downloaded the app again. This app has become a total disgrace. Its functionality is clumsy to the point I am afraid to touch anything in case it messes everything up. They have also taken away a lot of options and for some reason display your average weight on the graph which I see no benefit to. Doing some reading it sounds like Google has taken over the Fitbit app and just destroyed it. Brutal.

the food section is REALLY bad. it says I'm over budget and under budget at the same time (I've literally eaten 171cal thus far today), it thinks I'm somehow eating for tomorrow (date and time on watch and phone are correct), it thinks I didn't eat the previous day, can't get individual insight on meals, only for the entire day, caloric deficit is hidden in some menu somewhere i can't remember. its HORRIBLE and almost useless. only useful thing is that my calories are correct on home page.

I tried one of the workout sets for fat loss. For me, targeted weight makes me discouraged. Everyone should have a different weight that is not targeted. Second, not all workouts have video tutorials, even the basics. And it would be better if the video is from a real person, not 3D animated.

tldr: not worth it, even the free version. it has the makings of the good app but the free version did not convince me to buy the paid version. not many workouts, especially at home without weights, available. free version won't let you swap out exercises or log rest time. basic exercises and plans were okay, but only three workouts were available before you'd need a subscription.

Thanks for the reply, I didn't realize there was an option to create your own. Still wish some features weren't subscription only -- I suggest the developer integrate ads in the 2 minute rest period between sets. This would allow a steady income for the developer and a free option for those of us who don't want to do a subscription. Otherwise, it's great but not worth $6.99 a month for the rest of my LIFE.

the only reason I don't give 5 stars is because it does not have watch app for Android, only for iOS, but the app is great and the workouts are very easy to follow. I've been using it for two years now and I've seen the results immediately from the very first moment. Another improvement if suggest is the possibility to see both measurement systems (kg and lb) because most of the times they machines use different systems and it's very inconvenient to go to settings to change it everytime

I will give all the stars in the sky for this fitness app, it is very helpful for overall health management. It provides good guidance on diet, medicine, and physical training, making it useful for male & female of all age groups. The workout plans are easy to follow, and the health tips improve knowledge about proper exercise and lifestyle habits. Progress tracking and reminders help in maintaining regular fitness. Overall, it is an excellent app for fitness, health & diet.
I really like this app, but the exercises are limited. please add a more diverse listing, something a bit more modern. look at any social media app of those posting exercises and you'll get it.

This app is VERY misleading. It says it's free. However, after 3 workouts in any given category, you are forced to subscribe in order to move on to the next workout in the series. So much for a FREE workout app. With that being said, the workouts are very effective, and I love the app. I am just not prepared nor in a position to be able to subscribe..... I guess I am going to have to continue to search for a truly FREE workout app.

I really enjoy this app, as it provides a very detailed and convenient workout guides, which is really great! Couple of things that may be improved: 1. When in workout, switching to other apps, and open FitnessOnline again, exercises are grayed out and spinner under the header indicates that app is getting something from the internet before re-enabling. 2. Sync with Google Fit brings in 8 min of treadmill only as a walk, but not the rest of the workout exercises that pertain gym equipment.

There's not too many exercise choices without equipment. The great majority of what's listed is different kinds of planks, squats, push ups, and jumping/clapping. That might be enough for some people, but im looking for stuff that I don't normally think of off the top of my head. I'm trying to find stuff i can do to stay healthy without straining my chronic pain. Once I picked through all the choices that might work for me I was left with only 12 exercises. Other apps had more ideas than this.

This is the 1st app I paid to subscribe ever in my life. I downloaded several work-out apps and this is the best for gym work out. Highly recommend! I was new to gym work out, didn't know what to do, how to use machines or how to set up the routine. This app helped me big time to get into gym work out. Very informative and even fun! This is my day 15. Very satisfied. Except loading takes too long time. Please fix the app speed. And it would be nice if there's more various stretching.

I am very satisfied with all the features and the interface of this app. It covers all my needs. Very well thought, thank you! I would only recommend one thing: It would be great if the users could adjust pre-made workouts. For example, due to some issues I would like to remove a few exercises from the plan, or add extra exercise from available ones. I am still able to continue without doing some of them but when it comes to extras, I can't keep track what I had done extra.

Very good application. Perfect category of each muscle and the workouts for them. The only sad thing is that it doesn't count calories unless you're an upgraded user ( which is impossible for me to pay and upgrade because of payment system of the country that I'm living in). So I have to guess the calories I burn during my workout and it's not so accurate. But in general the app is pretty good!👏👍🏼 I like it!

Great app, nice trainings, but still lacking in some areas. Personalisation is one of the things to improve. Other apps offer trainings that adjust to current goals/weight, there is bigger control over what should be a focus of given workout, whether cardio should be added or not. Second point is the price. I can get apps for fraction of the price. Annual subscription of other apps costs same amount as one month of this app. It is definitely overpriced.

August 23. I enjoy the app and it's features. For me works great. Suggestions: 1) New machines are being builded every year. So expanding the library would be great as well. I'm not saying it's bad. It's huge and most of the times there is an alternative excercise. However, I've seen some in the gym they are not in the app that would be nice to have. 2) Would be nice an option with customisable templates for Diet without deleting the current selection.

Great app and fitness tracker. I use it for working out at home and the workout routines have been excellent and they challenge you more over time. In the past I had struggled at times to know what workout to do each day but this helps take away that stress. The only issue I have is being unable to sync it with Google health connect. I see no options for it under data sharing

By far the best fitness app out there for me. You select the workout, customise it, monitor and track your progress and includes info on how to perform a move with a clip of how it should be done. Doesn't bombard you with adds or sign up now. You can use these features without signing up too, only thing you cant adjust is rest time so workouts look longer than they are but subscribe to amend this. Awesome app, tested others and this one is worth subscribing too just to maintain the app.

I am using the free version and created my own workout which was very time consuming considering you need to enter sets, weight amount, etc. for each individual exercise. Unfortunately, when I begin to workout, it's very counterproductive! I have to stop and enter the reps that I did, the weight used, and why when I said what I wanted to do beforehand? The exercises do not manually go to the next one. It's just odd.

maybe i don't get it or im missing something. it wants me to start a workout i dont want to do today. today is shoulder day but it wont let me because it's forcing me to do a workout the app wants. it wont let me skip or re arrange the workouts. do i really have to pay to do this easy step?

I love the app and the variety of gym workouts you get, the diet, supplements recommendations as well as the community. The only issue I have is not being able to lock your previous day(s) workout the next day. For e.g on my cardio days I sometimes forget to lock my sessions because I would've went for an outdoor run and I leave my phone behind, when I get back I forget to lock the workout. I'll only remember the next day but then the app doesn't allow that 😫
Just Started. Easy interface. Needs upgrade of forcing me to get to the gym. :)

For the subscription app (which is not completely free), the tutorial and plan is good enough. But the fitur could be improved, especially for the timeline part. It shows notifications but where should we check it? We couldn't even see/check our pass post, need to scroll down til 2025 is finished. For the plan, I also hope that there will be more choices like for example, it says only for the perfect booty plan but for those beginners who don't wanna grow thighs and calves, it's a problem!

Good app with many exercises and short video animations. The huge problem is that it freezes for several seconds every time you jump from the list of exercises to the details of an specific exercise and back to the list. It constantly uses the internet connection to unlock every single screen you browse. If you have bad internet connection, the app will lock all the time. Another huge problem while doing a group of exercises is that you can't mark/highlight those that you already exercised.

I downloaded many workout trackers and this one is the best in terms of ease of use, available options and information. However, I wish I could edit completed workouts, because I made a mistake but couldn't go back and edit. Plus, I do different cardio on different days, so I'd love to be able to add that in addition to my saved workout.

I usually stick to popsugar workouts on YouTube but wanted to try something different, did download so many apps, they all were kind of annoying, especially when you need to pay for it. Then accidentally downloaded this app, and I must say, definitely it's Worth it, free workouts giving options for gym and home. Literally everything you would get for an app you need to pay for. Thank you so much who ever created this app. I am loving the different workout options that's available as well.
great app no adds, works as intended. keeps good track of progress and to be honest their monthly membership is a good price for starting to get gains or lose weight.
its very good this app, but I can't find where I change my password for my login I made login by Facebook but I would like to have acess with my email and a password

This app is great. I've been using it for almost 2 years. It shows you EXACTLY how to do a workout and gives you other variations to pick from. The app WILL insult you at the end of the workout if it thinks you didn't give it your all 🤣. I'm doing advance workouts to challenge myself,so I cannot do a lot of the heavy lifting yet that the app thinks I can do. So I do get insulted at times and other times I get praised. I love this app.

Been using this for like a year and some months now. It has workouts that works and suggestions that you can alter between. My main issue with the app is the suggestion or alternate exercises. Some of them may not be something that you're interested in doing or your gym/ home gym may not have available. When cycling through alternate exercises I just wish there was more than 5 so I can do a workout that I actually can do or enjoy doing.

Wouldn't recommend this app at all. I downloaded a workout and started. After 4 days of workout, it said, "Now I need a subscription to continue. When I was starting the workout, there was no way to figure this out, and now, after 4 days, it gives me this stupid surprise. Don't be shocked if you pay and half way into the workout, they ask you to pay more to continue

Wow! Wish I'd known about this app earlier! Just rejoined the gym after a number of years absence. All my confidence has gone and my first visit back to a quite "posey" gym, had me wandering around the gym not having a clue what to do and no plan or structure. I was about to sign up for some expensive personal training to give me a routine and some confidence, but after finding this app, now I don't need to! The recommended weights are brilliant as it's given me a guideline too!

I would recommend this gym app over any other one. I usually create my own workout routine and dont go off of already made one. This app, out of many that i have tried, has the biggest variety of exercises. I would just like it if they added a "Till failure" option (4 sets till failure) instead of needing to enter the exact number.

I like this app because it guides me on weight recommendations, helps to maintain an automatic log of the exercise and reps done. And if you get the paid versions then there are bonus options in terms of nutrition, supplements and other guidance you can receive from other trainers... Overall I would highly recommend this app.

Really great workout app! It gets a little bit getting used to especially to logging the sets you did. I just wish the rest time between sets isn't locked under subscription, that would've been really useful for free users to see how many seconda left until the next set

Great app with potential to be even better. Good exercise variety including functional and mobility exercises not available in most other apps. Nice exercise progressive overload settings with the ability to recommend target weight as pct of 1 rep max and ability to use pyramid sets. This app would be even better if the weight as a pct of your 1 rep max was automatically calculated and there was the ability to create your own exercises. I'm really looking forward to future updates.

I am currently on day 4 and i love the workouts the app recommends me to do! And on top of it, it's free! They have a variety of exercises and let's you know how many reps and sets per exercise unlike other apps. I am definitely keeping this and I feel a big difference on my body! Each exercise also shows you how to do correctly which is nice. 😊

it doesn't let me use a music app while using this app. every time the rest alarm goes off it shut off my music completely. if I turn off the alarm, it still shuts it off when the timer ends. also, if I'm in the app too long (less than a minute) it shuts off my music. if it's something that can't be fixed, I'd like a refund.

Used to be a great app, I even payed for the ad free version and how do they repay their PAYING customers that helped fund the app? By deleting all of the custom workouts that took hours to put together because they are now using a subscription-based service and want me to pay 10 bucks a month.. Uninstalling this app and would not recommend to anyone!

After using this app for a few months, I can confidently say it's the best fitness app I've tried. I really appreciate the level of customization it offers—even without a subscription - you can tailor your workouts to fit your goals. The detailed instructions for each exercise were especially helpful as a beginner, making it easy to learn proper form and technique. I’d love to see the exercise library expand even more, and I’m excited to see how the app continues to grow with future updates.

Overall, good. Always room for changes/improvement. The nutrition needs some improvement still. Food selections are limited when trying to keep track of everything. Doesn't have Cinnamon Rolls in the list, and if you receive prepared meals, those won't be listed too. Suggestion, adding more options and a barcorde/QR code scanner.

First 3 days of this app were absolutely brilliant, one of the best fitness apps I've used, then u get to day 4 and it blocks u and tells u to get a subscription. Really not helpful, I get needing a subscription for the meal plans etc but I don't want all that, I just want a fitness app.

Best workout app I've found. Wide range of workouts that are easy to follow and you can swap and change out certain exercises to suit your preferences or available equipment. Its very affordable too. My only negative comment would be you cant view the next workout until after you've completed the current one.

The app is very good. I am having trouble understanding the stimulation of the exercise especially if it is a static movement. The pictures don't help with how we should perform the exercise. Can the developer please fix. I am a beginner and it would help to see the movement performed clearly. Other than that it's pretty good so far.

Excellent choice overall. Also inexpensive. My only suggestion so far would be to add an option of lb or kg when inputing the weight after doing the set (instead of an app-wide setting), since at many gyms some machines are in lb and others in kg. This would eliminate the need for post-set calculations or constant unit switching in settings.

This app is absolutely amazing. Most apps like these are for home workouts or ones that don't require equipment. But this is for all the gym goers which is so hard to find in apps. As a student, this app is amazing that it's complete free to access pre planned workouts and plan your own. So glad I found this app!! Would definitely recommend. Its also great from categorising workouts into different body parts so easy to understand for beginners too!

1. It's absolutely ridiculous we can't add our own exercises if they don't have it in their catalog. 2. As I was adding exercises to my workout, I went to the settings page, then when I went back, none of my workouts were saved and I had to start all over. In 2025 that is ridiculously lacking functionality. Uninstalling.

I like the fact that it includes many equipment (you can change exercises if something is not in the gym), a detailed description of exercises with videos and recommended weights, there are levels from beginner to advanced, as well as different programs depending on the objectives (weight loss, weight gain).

"I really like this app; it is one of the best fitness apps I've used. However, there are far too many ads for the premium subscription. Please reduce the frequency of these ads or at least shorten their duration."

I was perfectly content with using this app for years, then yesterday, they put up a paywall. I could no longer access my routines or stats until I subscribed. They offer a "free four day trial", during which you can cancel to avoid the monthly charge, as long as it's a day before the trial ends (or something like that). Well, I signed up last night and cancelled about 12 hours later, only to be told by Google that my subscription will be closed after the point where I will incur a fee. I don't mind the ads, I've happily used this app for years, but there is nothing that warrants a $5/month subscription. Edit: it appears I did NOT get charged post cancellation, yet at least. It is still disappointing to see greed take over such a great app. Uninstalling after 2+ years of regular use.

This was a great app for tracking progress. I used it for a few year. I didn't use the routines, just the weights and rep tracking. Now I have to endure longer and longer ads each time I use the app unless I pay a monthly subscription. It started at 5 seconds of ads and loud music. Now it's up to 17 seconds each time I select a different workout. I will be choosing another app.

Love it so far!!! I'm using the customizable workouts. It's great that all moves are categorized by body part. I'm new to weight lifting and the diagrams and videos help me know that I'm doing all of the moves correctly. Also entering in my information when the workout is over is super easy. I just paid for the subscription so we'll see how the other parts work. I thought having the option to pay $4.99 a month was really useful. I personally don't like paying for a whole year upfront.

I really liked the options this app had for pre defined workouts. It also has a pretty good selection of specific exercises organized by muscle group. You can track your workout but there doesn't seem to be a way to define your own workout ahead of time. The videos demonstrating the exercises are nice to have for an amateur. The reason for only two stars is the ads, so many ads, and they are not just a bar on the screen. The worst ones are video ads you cannot dismiss for 10s and stop my music.

Deleted the app, this is a good starter app, but there are better out there. You have Shoulder exercises in Back, and so on. You have exercises missing, it constantly loses data so your progress is lost. There are 3 or 4 exercises that are repeated in each category. there is no way to change the order of the exercises listed..... this app costs over $50 it should be better.

Thank you very much for this app. It is very useful for tracking my progress in weights that I lift. I can see my progress. I have several suggestions to make this app better: (1) Cloud backup. I am afraid of losing my 3 months worth of progress data. (2) Switching mass units (kg - lb) on the exercise interface. I don't want to exit the exercise interface everytime just to switch units. (3) Progress charts. It will be very helpful if there are visual representations of progress.

I was using the app on a trial basis and it worked fine. I downloaded for the year and immediately started to have issues. I'm not showing as subscribed and when I try to enter custom exercises, it says I need to add permissions. I have already given the app unrestricted access, but I'm still having problems. I emailed the developers. That was a research project since they don't have support built into the app, I had to find a forum that would help with email. One month later and nothing

I liked using it just to see some workouts when I don't remember some movements for specific body parts. Only used it once or twice a week. But ever since they added ads to everything you click on.... I can't anymore. I'd like to go from movement to movement, and I try to measure my brakes. This just ads time to it. Not to mention, I usually have my headphones on and the music that blairs out over my own music is so damn loud. At least the music isn't bad though.

I have been using it for years. I like the simplicity without useless stuff added. I like the interface and that you can easily add your own exercise that are not in the database. I would love if they added a way to track volume (as a number next to the reps) for a given exercise so you can compare the volume your current workout to the one before.

I've used the free version of this app for quite afew years now and in the past Ive recommended it friends. However, I would not recommend it anymore, the ads are too intrusive, loud and you cant skip them. I understand that the developers might want to make more money but there are currently better free fitness tracking apps out there. I'm considering leaving.

Used to be good but they ruined it with the ad experience. I understand the ad at the beginning trying to get you to sign up but it happens LITERALLY EVERY TIME YOU CLICK INTO ANYTHING NEW. Oh, and the ads get longer each time and blare music over your own. It's also been glitchy and will just start blaring the ad music without showing the ad so you have to restart in order to get anywhere. After years of using this app, I will be leaving for something else. It's very frustrating and sad.
keep asking for a subscription...very annoying.. you just open an apps and without checking apps interface and what apps actually content it's just asking to buy the plan

Up until about a month a go this app had 5 star review. And then something happened where everytime I try to add info for different workouts, it's extremely slow. It's not my phone but seems like it's just this app. Not sure what happened to make it slow. Please fix and ill change my review back to 5 stars. The ads are terrible. Started as 5 seconds, now up to 13 seconds.

Half of the excersizes are not accessible unless you're a paid user. Moreover, a 30 second ad/reminder to buy premium plays between every excersize you open. You can't stop it, you can't skip it, you can't back out either - so if you chose the wrong excersize you get to wait for 30 seconds to just back out and then another 30 seconds when you get to what you actually meant to get to. And if you're just browsing, looking for STH new or just checking your stats - 30 second wait EVERY TIME

Paid 11.99 back on May 21, 2018 for full access but now there are ads and a subscription? I understand companies must generate revenue but here's 2 stars for it. If you're okay with the side ads and an occasional video, I do recommend the app otherwise you'll have to search further to find one without in-app purchases or subscription fees. Best of luck.

It's unusable. I paid for the premium version a couple years ago, but now they want subscription fees anyway. If you don't pay, it's unusable because of the ads/music that play every time you click anything. Read the other recent reviews, not the older ones from when the app was good. I use to love this app, but clearly things have changed.

Used to be good, I used to pay for it. Returned to working out recently, installed it again and it's just horrible with the constant ads. Very shortsighted move by the devs - yes, you might force a few more users to subscribe but at the cost of massive decline in overall active users due to plummeting ratings. Increase in revenue for one quarter doesn't seem worth losing a chunk of active users for life.

Great app even without subscription, but it's more or less a prebuilt, generic workout spreadsheet. The only improvement that I could see that would bring this to 5 stars is the ability/option to add notes to specific workouts that are tracked for that day. I often go to gyms at peak times and need to substitute a machine from free-weights, and it would be a good way to track why my tracking could seem off. Otherwise, great app, but there are similar/better options out there.

I would give this app a solid 4-5 stars, however, after a recent update, I have been experiencing performance issues. I'm not sure how to reach out to the app developers, so I'm putting rhis review here. Whenever I open a workout that I have a lot of history with (such as bench press or deadlifts), the app freezes up and is difficult for me to enter my progress. For context, I have multiple years of progress recorded for these excercises

Originally gave 5* but changing it to 2 because of the ads they added even if you're a paid subscriber. They interrupt your workout plus I believe there shouldn't be ads for paid subscribers. Great all around app if you're new to working out or the gym. It has many tools to guide you through a workout and keep track of the weights you're using.

Ads Galore I've had this app for at least a year now and it didn't have so many ads in the beginning. Now when you open it you're faced with annoying music BLASTED over your own when in the gym and typically have to endure it for 5 seconds before you can skip. It then is the same every time you click on an exercise to check the form. I will be finding another app to use from now on.

The best free app I've used. Yes, some parts need a payment but it has so many free videos that I don't need to pay for the additional videos. Maybe further down my fitness/muscle building journey I'll pay, but for now this fits me well. I love that I can choose an area I want to work, select the video and the demos are just there. Brilliant app, thank you.
i feel so comfortable about it

It's a very useful app for keeping track workout routines and progress. However, ads are very intrusive and constantly pop up, and recently the app has been asking permission to access my phone storage camera location and photos. All of which are not needed when tracking my progress. After denying and asking the app not to ask again, it consistently asks for it to have permission to keep asking me to turn on those services. Very annoying and shady, other than that it's works well and has lots of useful information. Just stop asking to access parts of my phone you don't need.

Really good app for being free. You get a lot more if you pay. Mostly videos that you can probably find straight know youtube. The scheduling makes absolutely no sense and is kind of rough to figure out. Otherwise, the app is great and teaches you all kinds of different workouts. Videos, pictures, and a written step by step description on how to do the exercise. I also like that it has pictures that show which part of your body the workout will target. Recommend for beginners.
it has a great experience but the reminder of premium is too annoying because every time you click on a workout it gonna show an premium ad for 10sec

I liked the app before the most recent update. There was a search function that was really great when creating your own workout plan (which is part of the free version). Everything else is great. The stock exercises are good, but adding custom exercises is amazing. The best part is the log of your workouts that shows when you pick into each exercise so you know exactly the weight you should aim for. Really great app if they bring back the search function.

Well I'm certainly no exercise professional and don't claim to be one, I have to torso of a adolescent boy. With that saud thus app is fine for day to day lifting but I just wish it listed more excercises. I was working on my back yesterday and although I found some of the exercises on the app there where others I didn't see like reverse butterfly or lawnmowers. Not a deal breaker, I do like recording my workouts on this app. Here's to going from zero to hero in 2019.

Do not use this app, you'll regret it! The free version started fine, but soon they added commercial. Every single time I check a workout, I got to swallow an advertisement. Every freaking time! But behold, that wasn't the worst, one morning all my progress, everything that I was keeping track, was gone! The garbage app replaced all my numbers with some decimal, say .17! I hate this app and never in million years using it again.

Like every other app I thought it would be useless, few seconds of app browsing was all it took for that perspective to change. Amazing delicious recipes and outstanding exercises for many muscles around the human body. I am certain that I will obtain guaranteed results with the help of my determination, diet, and exercice also not forgetting THIS APP, as without it I would be doing many exercises that could've potentially gave me negative results. Thank you for a great app.

Very limited App. Needs more varied exercises. Add more glute workouts. Add more resistance band workouts. Workouts should target more muscle groups. Could be revamped to be more interesting or challenging. This seems like an App for beginners who are just starting from scratch. Inputting data for your own weights and reps is cumbersome. Whenever you pause your workout during a time interval, it resets your workout and starts you back at set 1. Lacks the ability for you to skip rest times!

​I am a user based in Egypt, an excellent app. ​My rating of 3 stars is specifically tied to the subscription pricing. Given the current exchange rate, where $1 USD is approximately 59 EP, the subscription becomes prohibitively expensive. ​​I highly recommend that you consider implementing region-specific pricing or a purchasing power parity (PPP) model. This adjustment would make your subscriptions accessible to a much wider international audience, including users in emerging markets. thank you

Wonderful application for understanding workouts category wise. It always helps in understanding right positions, movements for each exercise with photos and videos. So whenever i start the workout of the day, I make plan with combination of 5-6 exercises of a particular category, for example chest or back. Then it always helps in proper planning and scheduling our exercises. Also it has lots of variety of exercises which can be done at home without machines.

Are there any reasons to use fitness apps? NO! I’m not a fan of all these useless training courses. I want just to lose weight and forget about training forever. So, in this app all the exercises I am interested in are blocked and should be prepaid. You lose clients, developers! I choose Sworkout and vote for its 30 days challenge.

Tromendous amount of training and well calibrated for my 6,1ft tall person. All videos are downloadable making this app more useful then others since it is also offline once the videos are on phone. I take away 2 stars because the app assumes training is only at gym and thus offers no home bodyweight training. You need a minimum of equipments at home to fully use this app.

I've been using this app for a year now. What I really love about this app is, it keeps me on pace. I use the built in timer so I'm in and out the gym in less than 35 minutes, when I'm not doing cardio. For ex. I set my sets to 5 I set the timer to exercise for 40 seconds per set and set the timer to rest 45 seconds between sets. This has saved me an exorbitant amount of time in the gym. No time to talk...

I know that I should press the x button after the ad, the issue is it opens the ali-express app after I press the >> after I watched the full ad. Also very disappointing that feedback section only allows photos and not videos like you stated in your reply...
I have enjoyed using this workout app more than most others in the past. It is simple and easy to use. Makes working out for beginners much easier when trying to figure out what exercises to do!

Finally a really handy app for preparing and following a workout routine. I have seen so many fitness apps, usually they are really poor unless you pay and still can be uneasy to start using them. This app has built-in workout programs and single workouts and a lot of exercises. Works really good also for custom workout and for just taking notes of the exercises done and keep track during a workout. The best part is that for Android all the workout programs & a are free and not too many ads.

Hi, I’ve been using your app for quite some time now and it’s been a great experience, really well done. I wanted to check whether your app supports integration with Android Health Connect. Specifically: – Can it read data from Health Connect (e.g., steps, heart rate, sleep)? – Can it write data back to Health Connect? – If supported, how do I enable the connection? Also, which data types are currently supported? If not currently supported, is it planned in your roadmap? Thanks.

Awesome app. I love how simple it is and that there's just a one time purchase for the premium version I do wish it had 1. EZ bar excercises. 2. Ability to edit past workouts. 3. CSV export for 1 week, 1 month, 1 years and all time. (because I'm a nerd and want to make my own graphs) without linking a google account.

They added options to add exercises not listed in the app. Been using for 2 or 3 years at this point and tried others in between, this is still my favorite by far. Simple and clean design, easy to use.

I would give it 6 stars instead of 5. It would be a sin to rate this app lower than 5 stars. If you want to work out with bodyweight, here they are. If you want to work out with a Handle Band, here they are. All exercises that exist are included. 💪
best apps i ever use for my progress tracking in gym. i used to be use an other aps (home workout) its really good too. The creator also likes to receive feedback from consumers
Can't do anything without watching an ad. Want to navigate through main menu - watch ad on every screen. Will search for other apps.
its a great workout app. if you are someone who likes to customize your workouts you can. There variety of workouts from bodyweight to equipment workouts.

it's good and I get the result what I wanted.I must thank my friend for suggesting this app.you guys can also can opt for this app if you are not finding time for a workout in the gym.This is a great app and no subscription is needed
