its very good. I am actually about to confirm wether if the app exercises give real results but I have confidence in their apps because up until now inwas using the one with no equipment and I saw results in 3 to 5 weeks
this app is totally amazing 😲. what i would suggest is adding a feature which allows u to take a picture of ur physique on a week 😉 so that u can also see the progress happening within.
Very happy so far, I love that I can set up my routine exactly to my equipment and capabilities.
Could have more predefined exercises and allow greater customization of new exercises (i cant put specifically abs for example) Otherwise great tracking app, free.
This is a very good app, you can use without any personal trainer, clearly demonstrated each and every exercises.
good apk but kindly add "auto workout counter" , it would be very beneficial i will certainly Add 5 stars but kindly add the feature in the app as you have added in the other app(home workout)
Yes, it deserves a 5-star rating. I love how you can customize the workout. Just started using it, but I'll be paying the premium purchase soon, the developers deserve it
very limited in what you can do for being a paid App. Most programed workouts have the same exercises.

excellent app to Kickstart your fitness journey at home with minimal equipment. App provides detailed,easy to understand instructions with tips for each exercise that makes a beginner's life easy.kudos to the developers

I've paid for subscription, only because I actually enjoy a gym app, love the plans and exercises and it's very easy to use. Can replace an exercise or remove it, very flexible and you will definitely find everything you need in your local gym to follow this up and get fit. 😁
I really like the app. Handles a lot of workouts. Just wish you added some exercises, like T bar rows

i love the app & bought ads free but since i completed the 30 days weight loss plan am unable to make chnages! keeps taking me to the completed plan and can only regenerate a new workout for everyday i want to adjust it to 3 days split instead of 5. UPDATE: deleted all personal data & started over. the app keeps generating a 5 days split despite choosing to workout 3 days/week while setting up the profile extremely frustrated that i got used to the interface
i still haven't started working out regularly cuz of some personal reasons but i can say that this app can fill up all the places for a private trainer for free.

Great app for beginners to intermediate BUT CUSTOM WORKOUTS problem Wish you could replace/ substitute exercises without losing the data (reps, weight). Example: I replace Bench press for Dumbell press... now my bench press weight and reps are gone even when I reselect it again. Solutions: Add option to save rep/ weight data Add option to fully copy and paste custom workout routine (having multiple variations)

I think this is the best gym app out there. It has programmes you can follow- tailored to your goals, or if you prefer, you can make up your own workouts with 100s of exercises to choose from. Each exercise has a video demonstration for reference. It tracks your progress with a graph and body map so you can make sure you are hitting all your muscle groups. It's so simple and easy to use and for £30 for the year, I think it's excellent value for money ❤️
I did not quite like the automated workout plans to my liking but they are great for beginners. I loved the create custom workout features though

Something went wrong with the app post cloud migration, It's not fetching the data, asking for update and wen clicked on update shows nothing to update, I might have lost history, even doesn't show the images of workouts... Edit - Above issue has been resolved immidietly by customer support.. Appreciate...
I am really enjoying using this app. I've been tracking all my gym workouts. it's very easy and user friendly. thanks a lot.

Great app! Just went premium. Time your workouts, track your progress, edit or turn off rest timer. Hundreds of stored workouts with animated movement examples and target muscle description. Customise workouts or use recommended workouts. Easy to use. You won't be disappointed.

For que I was a beginner, to today that I have more than 3 years working in bettering myself, this app was always with me, since day 1, I would be honest, I'm not the best on following routines, but Everytime I try to Train, I always get back to this app, someone said to me once that you'll always go back to where you were happy, and I can't deny it, This app have everything you need, from Training programs, to making your own by yourself, so.. Give it a try, and God bless you all, Happy life 💕

Best gym buddy app I've ever used! Simple to access and provides me with a vast library of exercises and workout routines for motivation and focus. Also helps me with my form with the little tutorial videos. Really impressed!
This app is amazing, absolutely helpful. Created a work out plan for me and so far it has been helpful and working as designed.

Couldn't get past an ad to get to the app after using it two days. Waste of workout time. Tried four times to get it to work. Just for the record, I tried multiple times to let the ad play out. But everytime it wouldn't allow me to move on. I well gladly sit through an ad, but really, three attempts and everytime I am stuck with no way to advance-not useful.

Perfect app for me. I'm using it for few years now, And I can say thay I am enjoying it. I can add custom exercises for my customized GZCLP workout as well. Ads are not that distracting. You can also support the app by removing the ads and paying a bit for it.
it's free and it doesn't have many ads! it's really amazing that they made this for free. as someone who doesn't have much money, this is awesome!

I’ve been using Hevy daily and it has made my workout tracking very easy. No need to remember sets, reps, or weights — everything is recorded properly. It really helps with progressive overload because I can clearly see my previous performance and try to improve each session. The app is very user-friendly and simple to navigate. Exercise info and structure are also good, which helps during workouts. Overall, a very useful app for anyone serious about gym consistency. Definitely recommended.

an absolute golden find for someone getting into workouts. so many routines to choose from, and I have built 4 custom routines for myself as well. you can browse through exercises, see actual videos to get the form right and also track your progress. this app has really helped me get on top of my workouts. can't recommend enough!!

This is one of the best apps I've come across. It's certainly the best one for tracking workouts. You can pick pre-loaded exercises or create your own. You can create your own workout routines. It then tracks weight, reps, and/or time. The free version is essentially the same as the Pro version. The main difference is that Free allows you to create and track three or four routines, whereas for $30 a year, you can have unlimited routines. Either way, it awesome. Highly recommended.

The app has been my constant while I workout for the last two years. It has some easy ways of tracking and also showing some wonderful graphs based on the data we share. I've reduced my fat and gained muscle. I'm a paid user. I've tracked my body measurements as well. It's quite good to see the changes in body tracked, measured and progress seen. Stay motivated, stay fit!

I've been using this app on my phone and watch for a few weeks. its a good app with decent user interface. were it falls short for me is: A) widgets on phone. my homescreen is 5 tiles across and their widget is 4, with no option to adjust length. B) on watch after I've started a workout, to allow you to see what you done the last two sessions on each exercise. c) to add suggested weight increases based on previous workout on each exercise, viewable on the workout itself after you've started.

so much is offered on the free version it's unbelievable, I will willingly pay for it because they deserve it for being so generous. today I discovered an option to compare your stats to another member so you can start competing with them which seems awesome. I love the community in this app. it reminds me a bit of fitocracy (who remembers that?) This is definitely the best of all the logging apps I've downloaded. maybe in the future we can also use it to track nutrition?

I use the monthly paid version ($2.99 a month), and it's exactly what I needed in a workout tracking app. Plenty of pre-loaded exercises with animations and how-to descriptions if you need a reminder, plus the ability to create custom exercises. I also enjoy being able to take a workout plan from the app and edit it as needed. Support is also responsive and timely!

Amazing app. Though I use the free version, it's a great app! Even allowing you to create your own workout. Love the effective tracking of time, love how the timer starts as soon as you hit the checkmark, and the loud ring that let's you know when your next set begins. Plenty of already built in workouts, and an encouraging concept to highlight workouts with a personal record badge. This app is well put together, and runs smoothly. No need for any workout app.

Excellent for strength tracking, better than anything I can imagine a paid app would offer. And it even has a free, perfectly set up Android Wear app too, which makes this already convenient app even better. I'm a bit of a novice, so being able to filter and see basic form guides for exercises helps me out also, although generally you only need to use them once. None of the paid features matter to me and unfortunately it's subscription only, otherwise I'd consider paying just to say thanks!

not perfect. it doesn't have every possible exercise or every variation, but it does allow you to create exercises, and though the creation method isn't %100 comprehensive, it works with most exercises. also, it won't allow you to put more than two exercises in a single superset, which is slightly annoying, but doesn't really affect the results. overall not bad, and helps track improvement. edit-just found that I can only save 4 workouts and create 7 exercises until hitting paywall. down 1 star.

Works exactly how I would like a gym tracker to work, which is high praise. The UI is intuitive and works well for both planned and ad-hoc workouts. I love that it makes tips and history for each exercise easy to access. And the way that phone and Pixel Watch sync is spot on – you can use both seamlessly. Only request is for heart rate to sync to Strava, and for workout details to go into the private notes field in Strava. Otherwise it's basically perfect 👏

I just found this app last week but it's so handy and I love it! It's absolutely worth the $2.99 a month to be able to add whatever custom exercises I want and make however many routines I want it's so user friendly and easy to track progress on. I absolutely adore it and couldn't recommend it more!

I like the app and the variety of exercises it can record, but it has a huge problem with syncing with healthy connect. I use Samsung Health, and sometimes the workout I did on hevy will not be recorded in Samsung Health negativity affecting my energy score, even with multiple attempts to troubleshoot the issue. Hevy plz fix the sync issue. I would give it a 5 star if it didn't have this problem

I have been using Hevy for about 6 months now. Rating it as a 4 (for now) due to the limited selection of exercises. I hope there is a way to add either more, or customize the workouts (exercise names, angles, setups, etc) so far I have been using the notes portion to remind me. Which is also a nice touch. The Strava integration is also excellent. Looking forward to more helpful features!

This is the best workout app by far. It's like Instagram but it doesn't rot your brain. Not only is it easy to use, accurate, and free; it also encourages you to keep going to the gym by tracking your workout streak. The app also offers short gifs showing proper workout form and the muscle groups beind worked in a given exercise. It even shows you tips on proper form, and allows you to leave notes for yourself. I personally use it to remind myself to focus on my form.

I used to rate this app at 5 stars, but now I'm giving it a 1 because of multiple bugs or other issues. I filed a support request but just received an AI-generated email. I often can't save workouts or even log exercises. Usually the app doesn't tell me why: it just silently fails to register the button press. Other times their servers seem to be down. When I save a workout, I might see a spinner for a long time.

I absolutely recommend this app, makes tracking my daily workouts super easy, you can see your progress easily, see stats, get monthly reports,... and no ads, no horrible paywalls for premium version (there is a premium version for this app, but the standard version is very comprehensive and super easy to use that it doesn't feel like you're missing out). will keep using this app, and keep telling all my friends about it.

Great App for tracking routines and workouts especially when it comes to progressive overload. My only complaint would be content wise. Lifting the same truck gets boring. Maybe an Oak Tree, 4,000 Large Pumpkins, or 100 Sofas. That way, it's more of a game as to what you unlocked that day. Otherwise, a very solid app. I've lowered my rating by one star because I've experienced a few bugs and there's no way to catalog an entire workout program.

I like that you can copy workouts. Modification is easy. I like being able to take notes and trending. My biggest issue is that something changed in the last month so that when I change a weight it will not let me complete the rep unless I log out and log back in. It makes logging more time consuming in real time. I use the free version so I just suck it up.

I've used Heavy since day one of my fitness journey and never looked back. The core experience is completely free,log unlimited workouts forever, with automatic lift and progression tracking for the last 3 months. No paywalls on what matters most. Want more? The paid subscription unlocks full lifetime statistics and additional custom exercises and routines. Great extras but totally optional. The free version is genuinely powerful,not a crippled demo. Heavy gives you the tools to track properly.

Great app, but needs more exercises and content: different grip handles for machines, exercise variations, rest-pause logging, maybe even logging partial reps, etc. It also needs to allow you to save past notes you submitted for a previous exercise without having to retype them (it shows a preview only)! Unfortunately I will probably have to move on to another app that has more content near the end of my subscription. Hevy is great, but it needs some love.

Started using this for the free version while getting back into weight lifting. used it for a year and love it. so easy to use. it provides a library of exercises to do for each muscle group and has videos of how to do it and where you should feel it so you can ensure you are doing it correctly. have recently signed up for the pro version as use it almost every day and now want to add in more than my 4 free routines. I know it is money well spent. would highly recommend for the free or pro vs

Just did my 150th workout and I'm really impressed with this user friendly app. The library of exercises is fairly wide and you can always add your own. Only recommendation is if when selecting which type of equipment you have (full gym, free weights, machine, etc) if you could specify any particular bars or special equipment. ie: trap bar, ez curl bar, etc. Otherwise, I recommend! Thanks guys!

I'm two weeks into using this app, and I absolutely love it. With a bit of effort I've been able to fully upload the weight resistance program I purchased and sort all the various routines (Bro split, PPL, and full body). The ability to crop videos to reference when creating custom exercises is super convenient. The timer works great, and being able to edit the routine or add exercises mid-lift is super intuitive and easy. Love the social aspect of it too. Super impressed.

This app is perfect, down to the last tiny detail. I started using it about a year ago and it's completely changed the way I workout, logging feels simple and straight forward, it's so much easier to know when your progressing, and just opening up your profile and seeing all the work outs you've done in the past is such a satisfying feeling, I just wish the app had more users!!

I really love this app for so many different reasons. Aside from logging workouts, there's a great variety of stats, little ribbons for every PR that act as a little dopamine hit, and these cute little graphics comparing your lifting volume to lifting helicopters, yachts, etc. I also love the social feature, because I can stay in touch with friends long distance and compare workouts! The paid version is worth every dollar in my opinion, and at $30CAD, the annual plan is pretty affordable!

I really enjoy using this app. It's user friendly and easy to log my workouts as I go. But you better hope you don't run into any problems because the customer support is next to non-existent. I've got a subscription issue that I've been trying to get resolved for 2 weeks. Out of the 5 plus emails I've sent them they replied to 1 telling me they see what the problem is but unfortunately that thread went cold. Super frustrating dealing with generated auto replies.

Having a plan going into the gym is important for me. This app helps me build that plan, and review past performance, in order to adjust and make improvements. Super easy to use and helps keep me on track and focused. I would love for the free version to allow more than 4 preset routines to be built by users, but with the "open workout" option, you can change it up when you need to from your primary setup. I like seeing the data it provides and sharing functionality.

Tried paid for a month. It is not intuitive, display is awkward, functions don't work. Then there's the Trainer. It spit out a poor version of push, pull, legs. The routines don't have enough per muscle group exercises to stimulate development. The sets have a very high rep range, increasing injury risk. It puts isolation work in between compound lifts, destabilizing and risking injury. It suggests exercises that don't recruit what you're working or have high risk of straining something else. 0*

Best app I've found... and yes, I pay for it! I've been lifting since 1996. Regardless, Hevy is a solid programme allowing one to use either recommended programmes or the ability to create a personal one. The ability to add exercises and sets is brilliant. One can search for an exercise by muscle group or equipment. App shows progress by time exercised and by volume - big plus! Downside: Extensive list is somewhat dated and body weight exercises are not included in volume and should be.

App is overall great and easy to use. being able to track my workouts and see what muscles are being targeted, helps keep me motivated. My only criticism, is that I wish they included more workout options. For example, ball ball or palates bar kit exercises. This app tends to be geared more towards those would have gym equipment or actually go to a gym. Still, it's an overall good app.

This is the app I always thought should exist, but I had no idea it would be better than my imaginary app. Truly, easy to use, easy to track my workouts, easy to see my schedule, easy for me to show off to my friends. I still have the free version, but I may upgrade soon. The free version is great, but there are a few more features in the paid version. Definitely recommend.

was looking for an app where I could log all my workouts including strength, runs, yoga, pilates, just for my own records! this allows me to track it all, not just strength like some of the alternatives. I've had a few small glitches recently where it's hard to mark my set as complete but I think this is just a small issue that will be fixed soon 🤞🏼🫶🏼

This app helped me so much with keeping track of my workouts, weights, routines and pretty much everything I had to do in the gym. It's genuinely a lifesaver and I love it to death! The free version does its job impeccably and I'm very glad that I found and managed to keep using it. I highly recommend it for anyone, especially those who take big breaks between gym sessions or who just want to get in the gym, turn of their brain and hit the weights on autopilot, noticing progress along the way (:

Love it. Setup routines ahead of time. Track reps, weights and sets as you go. Notes are visible right where you want them. The data from the previous workout is right on the screen so you can modify if needed. Rearrange or modify the routine on the fly if a station is not available or a different hit is wanted. Keep changes or discard them after. Very large exercise library as well the ability to add custom exercises. This is the perfect tool to use in the gym and when planning at home.

I absolutely love this app. It's given me a way to keep track of my lifts, build out plans, doesn't constantly ask me for money, and the free version does everything you need it to. Can take notes about my workouts, can add in modifiers, can even make your own excersizes if you know how to import the right files. Wouldn't have the progress I've made so far without it. Easy win dog.

I've used the free version of this app, for about 4yrs now. It has a large base of exercises and workouts. Options range from Free weights, machine weights, kettlebells, resistance, body weight, and cardio. All options have a "How to" for reference. It allows you to track workout time, Reps, Sets, weight, failure/warmup, supersets, has a section for notes, and you can save/view all prior workouts! I LOVE IT and I would highly recommend!!

The app is very user friendly and has many different exercises that can be tracked easily. It also has multiple helpful features to track exercises in further detail. However, there are some improvments to be made. Providing more details on which muscle is being worked out is needed. For eg, further categorising shoulder exercises into those that work out front, side and rear deltoids. Also, there are some missing exercises that are very beneficial and should be added. Overall excellent app

I've used a variety of workout tracking apps, and this is the best one I've used. I've been using this app exclusively to track my workouts for over a year now. It's worth paying for the upgrade a you can use to customize and build multiple routines. It keeps track of all of my workouts for every type of exercise and allows me to track the weight, sets, reps, and volume since I began using the app. I really like how customizable and easy it is to adjust my plans.

The app is really good, makes updating and tracking your progress workout to workout easy. My only complaint is that I wish their was a way to create a routine from your current workout, currently you'd have to remember your weight and rep ranges, plot them all down in the tracker, and save it. would be very convenient to just have an option added to the drop-down menu during your current workout, other than that though Hevy is great.

The best tracker app for real lifters. It's clean, ad-free design is simple and easy to use. I love the Routines feature, where you can create custom workouts with detailed sets, supersets, rest timers, and everything else. The database contains every exercise you can think of, and the insights after each workout are fun and rewarding. I recommend this app for anyone who wants to cut out the fluff in their lifting and wants to see real progress.

ISSUE: if you have exercises in your routine that you skipped, it still shows in the summary that you completed that exercise. Not great for tracking. No response from developers. RECOMMENDATIONS: 1. after entering one set, it would be nice for it to automatically jump to the next set and ready to enter the number of reps. just saves an extra tap. 2. ability to star exercises so that it's easier to find exercise used often.3. Give people a trial of the personalized trainers.

Had the app for 3 weeks and I'm very impressed with it. It's what I thought I would get with Zepp and the new Fitbit interface, but they failed miserably. Heavy allowed me to build my own routines for each day and then it auto fills your previous weights and reps each exercise so you aren't having to keep a log in a notebook. That allows me to push my limits and get stronger and grow my muscles. Highly recommended!

Hevy is the perfect app for what I'm looking for: a simple place to track sets and reps and monitor progress. The medals for PRs are a nice motivational touch as well. Probably the best thing about this app is its lack of ads and although there is a payed version, it doesn't feel like it's being shoved down our throats (looking at you, Strava!). I was recommended this app by a friend and have already recommended it to three other friends.

Love Hevy! It's so easy to build and follow a training plan. I can't fault this app, I initially used the free version but as my training plan grew I upgraded, I love how it tracks everything, shows me which muscles I've worked, shows how to execute the moves, has graphs to show progress, and tells me I've lifted a yacht 🤣 I think it'd be a great addition to anyone who trains, if you're just starting out or have been training for years.

Hevy is amazing! This is exactly the app me and my friend were looking for when trying to find somewhere to log our workouts together. The app is extremely simple to use and has so many useful functions. It's almost like Instagram as a gym-social app. What I love most is that there are no ads and no pop ups asking me to pay for any extras. Purely focused on helping you track your work outs with plenty of premade routines and the ability to make your own. Absolutely 10/10!

I'm a week/ 3 workouts into the free version of Hevy. The best part for me so far is the motivation of seeing other people showing up! If they can do it, I can too... it's fairly intuitive, I was able to set up my first weight lifting workout without a problem, and u can log other things like walking, yoga, skiing... I'm very happy so far. editing to add I've made it to 50 workouts and paid for a lifetime subscription! nothing but great things to say about Hevy app

I'm currently using the free version of the app and it's great. I love the features that we get just in the free version alone, since I'm a student who's trying to find a great workout app, this will be in the top list. App offer most things that we can usually use for workout routine, though it is limited for free. It's enough for me, so whoever need a try on workout app, try this and maybe go for the purchased version later on.. Which soon I might go for.

Great app. One small problem. I love this app. It makes tracking my workouts super easy and it plays well with my other fitness apps, however, the WearOS version drains my watch so fast. I don't know if it's tracking more than the Fitbit app does, but I can track a whole hour and a half workout with the Fitbit app, and still have enough battery to last the rest of the day, but with this app tracking my workouts, I'll get down to 15% and have to charge again before I go to work.

Love the app! its very easy to use and overall very user friendly. I sometimes find it hard to find the right machines in the app (especially ab machines). Id also appreciate if there was a feature where i could click on the machine in app & get instructions for how to use it & correct form. other than that it's great & id defo recommend it!!!

This app is really great. It was more than I expected. All plans and exercises are well sorted and cleverly planned. Exercises are planned such that you can do the next day's or following days' exercises (higher level) easily. It builds your stamina strategically; you don't even feel it until you move on for next day and discover that you could do the exercise smoothly. If you do the exercises sincerely, you'd feel the difference just within a week. I am high school student. And it perfectly fits my routine with its short yet efficient daily set of exercises.  All the bes, everyone. You must try this app for sure without giving any second thought.

The best app! Just ho for it! I have tried so many fitness apps but this is the best because even 5 mins workouts are available. And it doesn't let you see the future workouts until you do it which helps in becoming more consistent. So if you want to cultivate a habit of moving your body everyday, it's a really good app. it doesn't contain ads, most of the content is free

I love the app!! Its really helpful and even has different levels according to your needs. I recommend this for the people who are upset about their health. It reminds you to workout whenever you forget and also gives a day for rest. It also shows total calories you've burned according to the level and the workout you've done.I love it!!! Go for it !!! It will surely help you. All the best !!!🤗

Lately, I'm not able to even access the exercises as the ads won't let me exit out of them to the exercise. It won't even respond if I hit install, it just looks frozen. I've uninstalled and reinstalled the app but it's still doing this. edit: completely unusable now. guess I'm looking for a new app.

I really love this app. There's no intrusive ads and it has helped me lose a LOT of weight. I want to give it 5 stars. The only reason why I'm giving it 4 is because after using this so long, I wanted to upgrade to premium to support the creators. But when I looked at the cost, I saw that it was a $60 yearly subscription instead of just a one-time purchase. So, absolutely love the app and recommended to everybody but premium should be a one-time purchase or cost way less. I wanted to support!

It really is a great app. I've been using it since 2018 and I've never been so satisfied with an app, I have to reinstall it in my new phone. I've found what works for me and I will just keep on using it. The features I really loved are the rest days plotted and workout guides so you won't have to think about when to take a rest or how to do the workouts correctly. It even has the correct breathing while doing the workout. Adding, this has NO ADS at all and it's FREE. Very very satisfied. Kudos.

A huge problem I face is that when I tried to change the pace from miles to kilometers/hour, I didn't find the option in the app or in the Mi Band settings. What an issue! I hope the company fixes that issue because it's very annoying when I try to work out.

I'm almost 44. I've done this for 2 days. I haven't done exercises like this in a couple of years. The easy level is perfect for me. It's enough to challenge me but I don't hate it. And it's really short everyday. I skip the rest periods and it only takes like 5 minutes but I feel thoroughly exercised at the end.

Great free at home exercise app. I originally downloaded it about 10 years ago. I updated my phone and transferred the app. since, I hadn't used it in a while and thought I'd get back into it. when I opened it up I got confused and thought I had to pay for it, so changed my review unfairly. The app creator's responded kindly and said it was still free. I'm so grateful they did, because it's great. I have gone back to using it daily and can feel it working after a few days!!

this app is really great .I am able to work out consistently because of it. it gives me a new goal eveyday to achieve and it includes workout for every part of body like abs ,arms,legs and also a full body workout. It also tells us how to do this workout in anime and in video also. this app is excellent even for free.

The best challenge app. I changed my phone and was unable to download on that device and so forgot about it, but I stumbled across the app again on this new device and was stoked to see it was still available after all these years. Chur - Meitaki Ngao

used this app for 5 years now and I always reach my fitness goals. the videos and graphics are clear and the levels are appropriately scaled so you don't overwork yourself regardless if you're a beginner or a regular athlete

I've tried SO many different workout apps and this is one is by far the simpelst and yet most effective one. As someone who struggles to keep up a routine if it's too monotonous and repetitive, this app keeps me excited and interested because the combo and difficulty of all the exercises changes every day. It keeps me on my toes and challenges me just the right amount. And best of all, the main programs with SIX levels of difficulty, basically for 6 months, is completely FREE. I love it!

I love this app. This is my favorite fitness app. I don't need a gym membership with this. There's a demo of each exercise while you're doing them. There is a progression of the levels for each day of the month. Plus there are workouts for whatever body area you're targeting. There's every level from very beginner to well advanced workouts. There's a timer and you can pause the workouts if you want. I can't say enough good things about this app.

I used to like using the app with just the 30 day challenges, but I hate the new update. I don't like the new suggested workouts and the meal plan is a joke. I signed up for the free trial recently to try out the paid functions. When I tried to cancel, I searched the entire app, but I couln't figure out how to do it. Days later, I cancelled it through google play, but I had already been charged for the whole year. I emailed their customer service and never got a reply back. Don't get this app!

I've been using this app for 8 days now and so far love it. Very easy to use, it has a voice that tells you your exercises and how many when completing your workout. There are 5 workouts a day, and at first I thought that was small but doable. I have learned that those 5 workouts a day is perfect. Between walking a mile, on my own, a day and then doing my full body challenge my body is pushed at the end of it all. I love how it gradually adds more to your reps and mixes it up.

2025 review: I used to love this app but the data and cookie permissions are just ridiculous now and I've uninstalled it. 2018 review: Excellent app! It's progressive, with different levels, and you can choose which area you want to focus on. I've tried the full body work out and I like the fact that the exercises are varied and isn't boring. It also includes well thought out rest days, which are definitely needed sometimes! I wish there was a paid version of this app which is ad free.

This app is great for giving workouts that actually make me feel like I have accomplished something, and, the more I use this app, I'm sure I'll start seeing the difference I want. However, what I don't see, as with all fitness apps or the such, are stretching exercises that are just as important as the exercises. As, most people who use these types of apps don't know about stretching--something not taught in P.E. or otherwise. Please add this to decrease injury risk while using your app.

I absolutely love this app. The only thing is change, would be to show the day that you're on instead of percentage when viewing your challenges, since it'd be easier to see how far you are with each one. Otherwise, I recommend this app to anyone- I can fit the exercises in each day, they get progressively more difficult but not so drastically that you can't manage. I've tried many fitness apps but this is by far the best, nothing can compare!

This app has really helped me lose a lot of weight over the years of using it on and off. I would like it if we could exclude certain exercises from the routines though. I really hate burpees and don't want to do them but they're always included. I just end up skipping them but would like to remove them altogether. All in all, great as a free app and not too many ads.

I love this app! It's so easy to figure out and keeps me motivated to work out. You can choose types of work outs, what level you want (easy,medium, hard), it doesn't require weights, it gives you examples on an exercise if you don't know it, gives you an option to pause the work out or add a longer time to the resting period. Seriously great!

This is the best fitness app (free version) that I've found. It's been brilliant for home workouts in the school holidays when I've got children home and I can pick as many challenges as I have time for. It's simple to use, the format is user-friendly and the cues during exercises are helpful. Its got me in a great exercise routine and the rest days are spot on. I adapt the exercises to suit me, eg. I can't do press-ups so do them on my knees but I add weights to the exercises I find easier.
Toda hora pede para colocar senha.

In terms of what it's supposed to do, this app is great. I would give it a 5 out of 5 stars. There are different levels and different exercise types so it suits different needs. The full body beginner pack starts off easy, making it easier to build a habit of exercising. I particularly like how if we miss a day, it wont show as a big red cross, but it rather accumulates the days we exercised on the calendar. The only reason that I am not giving a 5 star rating is because of an ad I saw just now. I am not sure if you have control over what ads are shown, or if Google or a third party does, but the content of the ad was sexually inappropriate. Otherwise, this is the best exercise app I have used in the past 3 years for building a habit of exercising.

This is the best app for me. I've tried gyms and programs.and they all failed, due to my busy life style. I roll out of bed and do the workout. Sometimes I do a second one the same day. It's well paced and easy to follow. It focuses on consistency, which is key to get in shape. However, I am not too keen about a recent update. the weight graph and the calendar tracker are on the fritz. Since I use both frequently it interferes with my tracking.

Great Excercise App. I love that you can do it while watching TV, listening to audiobook, etc. Most of the apps requires undivided attention while exercising but this one allows to do whatever you want while completing the daily routine. I'm not great, I only did one 30-day challenge at medium level but I can see the results without making any other dietary changes in my daily routine. Definitely recommend it to try.

It's the only fitness app I've used so far and it worked great for me. Starting from easy and reaching hard takes months but it is worth it. I didn't want to lose weight just get back in shape and strengthen my muscles. And in 2 months time I got the results I wanted. It is a great app. Ads pop on just in then end of the program so they're not annoying. It does have instructions and links to videos for exercises. Overall is a really good app!

What an awesome motivational exercise app! You set the difficulty level and each day the app gives you daily exercises to do. It also has a meal planner in the free version suggesting meals and snacks daily. I've been inactive (230lb), and needed something to get me going again. The only drawback with free version is ads. You can pay for pro if you don't want them. UPDATE: I've completed the first 30 days and I can already feel and see a difference! Can't wait see results in next 30 days.

The ads come on before the exercise routine has ended. This is really frustrating when you're doing a plank, and need to hear the countdown, but all you get is an ad. I'll pay a couple of bucks to go ad-free. That should be an option. The reminders are glitched. Only one reminder is allowed at a time even though you're allowed to set more than one.

I train regularly , but it's always good to keep your body guessing, saw this app & thought I'd give it a try. The selection in training certain parts of the body is good, & the easy, medium, hard choices. It has audio & visual so very good if you're not looking at the phone You don't need much space & if you have a mat you're good to go. First few days the set of exercises are short, I often do 3 sets until they get longer. I like it so far.. and have already shared with my friends.

This is a great app,truly efficient,keeps track of fitness,it's all around a good app,would highly recommend it,I'm at day 5 of some of my workouts and I'm feeling the heat,without a doubt a good app,but there is one thing that is needed in every workout routine,stretching.I don't know if they come in later stages,or never,but they are very needed in helping with a good workout set-up. It would be best if you added a few more of those.Otherwise,a fantastic workout app.

This app is awesome! The only thing I do not like is if I exit the app for a brief moment to answer a text or change my music, the rest timers do not continue to count the time between exercises. It definitely works if you stick with it! I love how they change up the exercises each day to target different muscle areas. I finished the medium 1 ab work out and I'm already starting to cut out. It's an amazing. So glad I found this app, it's changed my life.

Honestly this app is the reason how I went from 68kg (Dec2018) to 58kg (Oct2019). If you stay dedicated and just do it as often as you can, even for a mere 5 minutes is good enough it's effort and a step forward anyway. It's great my body is a lot leaner and I like the different workouts they provide it definitely feels like the app knows the kind of progress my body is going through. I just have one issue, currently when I look at the monthly reports the days I work out aren't showing up.
I have been using this app since 2016 and find it very convenient. no fees or anything,just minimal ads and you are sorted for your basic home workout

The first 2 weeks were great! I like that the exercise routines changed daily. After the 2 weeks or so, the same 2 exercises are repeated every single time! Those are butt bridges ans superman. I am so sick and tired of those exercises. There isn't a day that doesn't go by that those 2 exercises don't show up. I will finish the 30 day challenge, then I'll be done with this app.

-2018: Wounderful short workouts. Did not expect to be challenged by the app but I was wrong and definitely feel the burn everyday. It progresses at a reasonable speed so the workouts are progressive and sustainable as long as the correct difficulty is chosen. Medium is harder than it looks. Don't be afraid to try the easy level first. 2020- Still using this app. Missed a year of working out. Back at easy level. Still a great app for 30 day challenges.

Love it! I like that you can pick which body part you want to exercise or full body. They have various difficulty levels to get training targeted to your level on each muscle group. The app is so easy to use and the workouts offer additional options during like skipping an exercise or skipping a rest and giving you tips to make the exercise harder or easier while you are in the pose 🙂.

first time using one of these fitness apps. its actually pretty great , having a structured plan to workout and in a short time frame. a much needed tool in this busy life. i always stop working out cuz studies cuz it takes a lot of time, time i can spend studying. but with this app i only need to spend a few minutes, at least with the easy plan. lets see how it goes.

This app works great. The workouts are straight forward and we'll balanced. I haven't tried the paid service yet. But I really like what I have done so far. The ads aren't too intrusive and don't bother me. They seem pretty well targeted lol. I have been getting some good results and once I do all the challenges I am considering the paid option that makes a special workout plan just for you.

I got this app, and even paid the $9.99 for no ads and such, cuz I was thinking it is pretty much worth it , (if it works). Three days later and things are going great! Already lost a few pounds! Anyways onto the cons...  So when I did the chest workout today it made me do backward lunges.. Now I am not a physical therapist by any means but last I checked that didn't have anything to do with chest? When I first started using this app it didn't warn me how fast things would be moving forward. You go through these exercise sessions within 10 or less minutes! There was no warning as to how fast and intense it was going to be. Granted, three days later I got used to it, and it has probably helped amazingly! One thing I would change is to at least let people know what they are getting into.   I love how there are animations showing how to do the exercises, and videos if you need more detail! I think this is the smartest and most amazing thing the devs have done! This app also has a meal plan, and the diet may seem barebones at first, but then I realized this is probably what an actual normal person eats lol.  I would give 5 stars but there was that issue with wrong exercises! 8/10
I signed up for a 7 DAY FREE TRAIL just now and it charged my account for the whole year!!! That is not ok! You need to refund my money right now! Not off to a great start at all!

I love the app, it is super easy to use, I have just finished the Hard level of abs 30 days and I'm on day 16 for Hard level of the Butt workout. The only  suggestion for improvement is maybe instead of getting a notification  saying that you should exercise, the notification can roughly estimate the time it will take you to complete the exercise for that day. Just something I'd like to see, to get motivated EVERY day. We all know it is hard to stay continuously motivated so anything you can do to change that would be perfect.

Pretty useful and straightforward app overall. I've only tried their full body workout material so I can't speak for the rest of their workouts. I think the instructional videos are a great addition as sometimes it's difficult to figure out the movements from only a picture. However, I wish there was consistent information about the exercises we were doing. If you click on details, some exercises do mention what part of the body they're targeting, some tips to do it well but not all.

It's free and it helps you decide what to do when you're too busy to create your own workout plan. I totally love the full body workout option. The moves are repetitive so it gets boring after awhile, but it targetted every muscle group without having to buy any equipment. (you will need a mat but that's a very very basic requirement).

I actually use this app quite a bit. Between this app on the highest difficulty for full body workout and using myfitnesspal app, I lost 20lbs in 30 days. Now I use it for warm ups and cool downs. I like that you get more than 3 options for difficulty settings so you can work your way up. All the exercises are clearly explained and demonstrated in the app too which is nice

Fantastic app! This is the first app I've been able to stick to purely due how quick the sessions are and the ability to choose a focus area. I've been doing the full body, arms and booty 4 days a week and then abs and legs whenever I feel I need a little extra, and it's super great. They progress harder/longer as you go but it's not super noticeable as it kinda works with your progress. Ads are only at the end of each session and very quick. You don't need any equipment either!

Exercise is my hobby. I'm an avid with weight and cardio. I downloaded this app because when it's cold and snowing outside it's hard for me to motivate myself to leave the house - even for the gym. I'm very happy with the selection of workouts at the hard levels and think the ease of use would be great even for a beginner at easy levels. I enjoy the experience of having another day highlighted as finished - it motivates me to do it more often, even when I don't really need to.

The exercises are very well though, you can choose the difficulty level: easy, medium and difficult. You can also choose the body parts you want to work: legs, arms, butt, abs or full body. It's really fantastic at it really works, during the exercise you actually feel the muscle that you work. Just perfect! Thank you for this app, the best for body workout at home, no needs of equipment and everything is free and well explained.

the app is great and I love the exercises, however I just recently got a new phone and when I went to download the app on my new phone I noticed none of my progress switched over so now I have to start all over again smh. There should be some kinda system that saves ur progress in case of situations like this, seems like a huge oversight...

I highly recommend this app to the person who is ready to start a habit but struggles with the high expectations of other options. The easy plan starts out soooooo low. Then it increases weekly, but only by a little. There are pictures and timers to help you. You can redo any day, if you aren't ready for more. It literally took 2 or 3 minutes to finish it. I had a huge mental block of exercise being too hard or taking too much time. This was exactly what I needed to build success!

I love this app, ive been using it for around 2 years and i pay for the full version. 3 days ago it had an update, and now every time i liad the app error 103 displays and then goes. What does this mean?

30 day ADDICTION more like! I have been following the FREE 30 challenges for a while now. I haven't signed to the plan as all I needed as ideas on exercise types. Starting at easy 1, only 4 - 6 minutes per category (I complete all 5 categories) to begin and build my self up. The easy 1 stage takes 20 - 30 minutes to complete it all and is simple, easy to follow moves. I have all my friend using this app also!

The app itself is pretty good but I feel like the workouts, especially the full body ones, are not that thought through. They place way too much emphasis on the legs (many "full body" workouts are literally like 50% leg exercises) and the order in which you're supposed to do the exercises often doesn't make much sense either.

I'm not sure why the app even exists if I can only use the app to view data and not edit it. I can view the information directly on my wrist already. I need to be able to modify the name of the workout so that I can keep track of my workout routines in the app. I was able to do this in the Zepp Life app and all other fitness tracker apps I've had for the last 10 yrs. Mi Fitness is failing to sync with Zepp Life, so I cannot document my workouts there either. No tech support in the US.

I was super happy with the app as my Xiaomi Jump Rope and Sport Bracelet 10 connected and shared data together. To continue my body analysis in more detail, I've ordered the Xiaomi Body Composition S400 scale (even though I already had another working brand) and just found out, that it does not allow data transfer from Xiaomi Home to Mi Fitness,and have to type all my data manually every day. You should have a full bridge or allow one app to serve all, what on Earth leads to such a decision??

I just got a new phone and with it the newest version of the app, and the sleep graph is gone! It no longer shows how many times I woke up or for how long. I used that info, please bring it back! Get rid of the "sleep routine" if you want to remove something, it isn't at all accurate. But bring back the graph!!!? Edit: I noticed it also doesn't show my sleep cycles at all. it did notice I woke up twice, but it showed my entire night's sleep as 3 naps! What a mess

Forced to switch from Zepp Life to Mi Fitness when I upgraded my Mi Band 5 (which is still great aside from a dying battery) to a Band 9. It's a shame. There are some oversights that I can live with: You CAN'T log weight in pounds, or distance in miles; the sleep graph sucks. But the major problem is a severe background battery drain that appears after a few weeks of use. The only fix is to reinstall the app and configure it all again. I've missed alarms b/c this app drained my phone overnight.

The app displays everything in widgets on the main dashboard, which itself is presented well enouy. It updates quickly and there have been no problems synching with the band (Mi band 9). However, the dashboard looks a bit crude and I don't like the method used to display the HR, SPO2 or the stress data. The display in these areas is poor and unclear. It doesn't seem to matter how tight the band is on my wrist, the displayed metrics don't show the data clearly enough.

The hardware is great, but the app is terrible. It thinks I am asleep when I am watching tv, and it does not allow corrections to sleep times. So it tells me I have terrible sleep because it is too long, and I wake up several times - it makes sense since I am not asleep! with fitbit you can easily correct the sleep timing. It is not a hard thing to implement in an app

There is a major issue in a fitness tracker that can NOT measure calories appropriately. My average daily caloric consumption is somehow 900kcal :| I made sure I reviewed age, weight, height, and still the Xiaomi Watch S4, keeps my total calories wrong, even though it is measuring my heart rate the entire day. You need to seriously review this! Plus, is not syncing data to Health Connect, another minus point.

A lot of the measurements are at best approximate, and sometimes way off. Today I did a fast-paced treadmill: 7 kms per hour, for 53 minutes. The app recorded less than 2 Kms... Yesterday's measurements were far worse. Really disappointed, using the app with the best Xiaomi 14 ultra, latest watch. Not sticking with Xiaomi if they cannot do precision.

Good app, there are some easy improvements to be made: 1) Weight trend line looks almost flat, since the scale covered is from 0 to around your weight. Would be easier to read if +-10 kg around your weight is displayed. Or if there was an option to control time/weight value range for the graph. 2) Some workouts kcal are overrated (walking), while some others are underrated (cycling). Overall quite intuitive app, easy to use.

The App is good 👍, but it needs more improvements. Better charting with more options, more interactive. More exercises in the App itself. More fitness tools, like training timers, countdown timer. Maybe add a fitness, gym exercise library. Add the Xiaomi mi body composition scale to the App, to make the App all in one App, then I would give it a five ⭐ s.

Need Auto Pause Feature for stopping or slowing down during Outdoor Running Workouts (traffic lights, drinking water, crossing roads). Also needs a detector in case you stop running by any reason, to stop the clock and stop counting pace and times etc. Also please add option to Edit and FIX specific Km in a Run - like fixing minutes, pace, time - per Kilometer in case the GPS was wrong or you forgot to stop the clock!

this app leaves much to be desired and is no longer reliable. Recently when I start a workout it says that the time is 0 seconds. Which means it's either not recorded or it's recorded with my stats but with zero time. Which affects the overall time spent working out and my heart rates etc. It's very disappointing that this app no longer works in the background. I haven't changed any settings... this app just sucks now. and you also can't edit a workout either

Heads up before buying: This app does not allow you to change units of measurement. You are forced to use kg and km, with no option to switch to lbs or mph. This is a standard feature in almost every other fitness app. Xiaomi, please add an option for imperial units—it would make a huge difference for many users.
it's good, and really working, also good animation

I've had no issues with this app nor the device connected to it. Only issue I have is the band protection notification that pops up when I open it. I have tried following its directions but my phone doesn't have what it says so that I can stop the notification. otherwise this app is great. the device connected to it lasts 10 days before needing to be charged.

This app is so bad I'm going to sell my watch which I really like. The app has no auto workout detection, you cannot connect it to any bathroom scales apart from the Xiaomi one, you cannot manually add weight in pounds only kg's, the smell tracking is the worst I've ever seen and not worth even looking at. I'm now going back to Samsung Fit3 purely because the app works.

I have a Xiaomi 11T Pro along with a Redmi Watch 5, and it has issues. The DND sync function does not work. When I turn it on, on my phone, it does not turn on, on my watch, and vice versa. Also, on the watch if the button is accidentally pressed, and it shows me the list of all apps, it stays on that page. So if a few seconds later I want to check what the time is, I can't, because even after I lift my arm, it shows me a list of apps, and if I don't have free hands I can't check the time.

I need to clear data on the app daily (specifically right after I was up) for heart rate data to sync with Google fit, sleep data and blood oxygen always get synced and will continue to do though throughout the day, but heart rate either stops right. Update: I noticed that the app is now being weird, it will say I got X hours of sleep, but when I open it to see sleep details it tells me there's no data, even though I health connect it shows me the sleep data it got from mi fitness

The app is great The platform is solid. The only reason I don't give it five stars is because it doesn't have a setting to change from the metric system to the impirical system. I believe it uses the region setting on the phone and multiple resets hasn't fixed it. A manual override in the app would be appreciated. on the brighter side I am learning the metric system something US public schools failed me in.

It used to work great but now the blood oxygen and stress no longer show. I've set them to all day tracking but it still won't display anything, just keeps asking me to wear the device for measurements. I never take it off except for showering so how that's supposed to work is beyond me. Manual measurements still work though.

so far it's not buggy which is good but it's not very thought of well and not organised, for example there's a voice recording function on the watch S4 but I can't get the file from the watch at all... so what's the use of the mic and function?? I can't remove watch faces so that it doesn't take up space and I would have to scroll all the applied watch faces and scroll through what I actually want... the Xiaomi pay has not been working till now....

The app is annoying and frustrating. You are unable to edit and add any info. Can't add activities, adjust sleep, not even your menstrual cycle can de changed! Often times I am just watching TV or lying in bed and it registers as sleep - that is more of a band problem...

The app does not allow for more personalized data and (I might be wrong about this) appears to be based on the biometrics on a "small man". I am a woman so I don't feel the feedback is very accurate. I was interested in the sleep tracking which seems to be way off.

Smart watches + app work, but health stats are not very informative. It supposed to measure PAI, it doesn't. It can't distinguish chill working from intense running or workout despite having heartbeat data It is health monitoring in the name only, but gives you some basic estimates of how many steps did you walk.

not the best by a long shot. constantly says couldn't sync data. I have searched Xiaomi help website but what they recommend doesn't help or doesn't apply to my phone. I have sent an email asking for help but no response at all. Shame really because the watch/fitness trackers is very well made, support is a let down

The app is good and the watch itself is also but the most annoying thing since the new update is that it is vibrating every 5 seconds when the phone is charging and a then few times a day it is uploading data an so on...is there a solution for this how can I turn it off and if not can I somehow return it to the last version? I will rate it more postivie when the problem is fixed.

Lots of bugs, inconvenient, poorly tested by developers, illogical. For example, to enter a new weight, you start by scrolling almost thirty kilograms. The question for the developers is, why not start from zero? That would be better. Sarcasm.

100 different band displays to choose from but ALL are in the American date format. The app refuses to notify phone calls without also having contacts & call logs permissions to snoop on. Reading the fine print reveals this Chinese app is collecting every bit of data about you, your contacts and texts. Only give it the permissions it needs.

Moved to Mi Fitness after replacing my Mi Band 6 with a Mi Band 10. So many issues compared to Zepp Life! - DST breaks the database rendering all data prior to the clock change inaccessible - cannot edit wake up time in app, only bed time - no integration with Mi smart scale S400, data must be input manually from the Mi Home app (no Google Fit integration) Nice hardware but the app is an extreme letdown. This will be my last Xiaomi fitness product.

Great Brand but useless Product especially this Xiaomi Smart Band 9 Active. Ive been using this for the last 7months for almost everyday and it keeps on lagging, the screen is difficult to touch, now the screen is blinking and its consuming battery. I never wear it when ill taking shower or swimming. I contacted the Seller but the place where given to replace it bcoz its still cover for the 1 year warranty is way way far.

Unfortunately doesn't connect to mi body composition scales, sync with Zepp Life app often reports no data meaning you always have to sync itv 3 or 4 times before the data transfers. Would be 5 stars apart from that. They should warn you about the Mi body scale issue before you buy a Mi band 9.
really great workouts, I break a sweat the great majority of the time.

Everything was working fine with my Redmi Watch 5 Active until the recent updates. After installing the latest version of the app, the “Receive notifications only when phone is locked” setting no longer works as expected. Even with the option enabled, I still receive notifications on my watch while actively using my phone. I hope this issue gets fixed soon.

Redmi Watch 3 Active: It should automatically recognize walks, display album art for music, and measure stress using a Smart mode. It doesn't open notifications on my phone, and I have a Xiaomi 14T. It's not recognized in Xiaomi Smart Hub mode, and it should be there. For now, fixing that would be ideal since it's a device from the same ecosystem! Disappointing!

Edit 2026/04/20 Still the worse. Despite switching off all permissions, still tracking steps on mobile app. You can never get the real step count with this app syncing. Tried everything. The only way is to uninstall it and keep using the band without sync, which results in no historical data!

This is everything that is wrong with modern devices. I buy a god damn piece of hardware, and I have to install an "app". Then they force me to make an account even though I don't want or need one. I try to log in with Google and I somehow still have to enter a password (do you not understand the concept?) I try to enter a password but it's "too long", so I enter an insecure password, now there's a captcha. Get a better brand.

Forced to update app. Lost everything on my mi band 8 pro from all previous workouts. Keeps saying unsupported from all the different regions I've tried. I've wasted many hours and tried so many different options given on the web. No longer a fitness tracker. The same has happened to my son's mi band 8 Pro. we are both devastated

it's functional it does what you expect from the product but nothing more Edit: after the last update it also mirrors certain notifications you don't want mirrored and gives you no options to turn it off. for example: I don't need the notification that I am currently calling someone on my watch when the watch doesn't have a call function. and if you have no service the notification that you don't have service constantly makes the watch vibrate. no option to turn these notifications off.

The features are great, especially the sleep tracker. The only fault I've found is there is no functionality to sync my data with other apps. I would like to sync my with Samsungs 'Health Connect', but it does not read or write data correctly
the app is okay, but the device I have does not connect well so only tracks some of the data intermittently. The weather does not load, and there have been no updates or fixes for the known issues.

really good. it works really well and handles everything nicely. EVERYTHING. only has a bug where it is constantly vibrating to guide me when I'm using maps. I had to turn off not recieving maps notifications. apart from that Easy to use, connects well, reports all I need, looks wonderful too

The app doesn't have an option of turning on "Events". There's events app on my watch and when i open it it says "Turn on Events in the app". But in Mi Fitness there's no Events app. Have you removed it on purpose? It's impossible to sync my calendar with the watch and that's very inconvenient

after updating my phone to the latest version (HyperOS 3) autostart for this app has completely stopped working. it was automatically turned off after the phone restarted. other than that, notification for this app also stopped showing up. it made things harder to know if your watch was connected or not. please fix this. this is urgent.

With the latest update (24 Mar) the app constantly spams me with notifications on my Mi Band whenever I'm charging my phone. For every percent charged I receive at least a couple notificationa. And I get notified constantly for useless things like turning my Wifi or 5G off. No matter what settings you play with on your phone or this app you keep getting spammed with notifications. The only way to stop this is to completely restrict all notifications from the app, which makes the Mi Band useless.

This was a great purchase. I bought a Garmin and while the feature list was long I found the tracking for sleep poor. I wasn't interested in sleep tracking any more but I then realised it was doing a better job than Garmin Fenix 8. My expectations were minimal for this watch but they have definitely surprised me with the quality of the app, UX of both app and watch. I would highly recommend for anyone that wants a tracker that just does what it's supposed to do because this might give you more!

Doesn't sync with strava, for some reason. I've been using this app for almost 2 years now but recently, i noticed that it doesn't sync any of my activities from my xiaomi smart band and mi fitness app to strava anymore. My internet connection is stable and both Mi Fitness app ang Strava are updated tothe latest version. I tried to disconnect my mi fitness account to strava and reconnect it, but still it doesn't sync.

latest update on my redmi watch 5 is buggy. Everytime I call someone the watch vibrates and vibrates again when I hang up. Also sometimes my cycling data records the distance but not the map route. Also had a long-standing issue of the app asking to connect the phone to the watch even though it is connected. This happens mostly on my Motorola phones.

On pixel 8 it keeps displaying an error "band connection protection" to keep it running in the background. However the instructions given don't provide a solution. The workout section for history could be improved, but other than that the app works great.

A new serious bug in this update. My phone has a running timer just started and now my watch is continuously reporting its progress. Nothing else works now as I cannot dismiss these notifications, which will continue until the timer ends in 8 hours 😡 I had to turn off app notifications to get some peace!

Cycling, after pausing (auto or manual) the map is not displayed, but the distance count is still recorded. Run or Walk, when syncing to Strava, some data is not included, such as number of steps, cadence and stride😭. It's difficult to export GPX. HR data, etc. are not included.

I have had the Samsung Galaxy Watch 3 for years, and it's been pretty okay, but it's trying to be 7,000 different things that it doesn't need to be. Same for their app. This device, and this accompanying app, are simple, to the point, and easy to use. If you're looking to have HAL 9000 to wear on your wrist and control on your phone, don't get this. But if you're looking for a fitness and time focused watch with simple phone integration, this gets my thumbs up.

Just finished my Day 7 and I am enjoying this app so far. I love that it's free and doesn't force you to subscribe. The duration for every exercise of 10-15 minutes is also enough for me as a beginner in pilates and doesn't put a lot of pressure in me. The countdown and voice over feature is also helpful during the exercise. lastly, the app's interface is also easy to navigate. Keep it up, devs!

Great app especially for FREE. The music is relaxing. I only wish I could play my own during the workout I love the countdown and halfway point features so I don't have to jump back and forth to my phone timer. Most exercise apps don't give you a rest between each exercise. But this one also has the option to skip it each time. I also love that it is a quick wrap-up when done, no having to check off each exercise individually at any time.

Free trial - isn't actually free. I wasn't let to try something for free and then take a subscription when I find it worth it. I was charged money up front for the next month. I let it go and cancelled from my payment mode for the next month since I felt cheated, and guess what, I was charged a humongous amount again. and then there is no way in Google play or the app to cancel the damn subscription. it doesn't mention anywhere that I am subscribed to something - monthly or yearly , just deducts

I'm actually surprised at how good this app is. I'm using the free version and the workouts are doable in a living room with a toddler present- so it goes to show how easy it is to fit into your life. Max amount of time I think is maybe 30 minutes, but the work ours I've been doing are between 10 and 15 minutes. Perfect to ease into moving your body if you've not done it in a while.

Pretty good so far, no complaints. I'm on day 6 so I haven't finished yet but I'm enjoying it, exercises are somewhat fun (as fun as breaking up a sweat can be lol) I haven't seen any noticable progress yet but then again I'm only on day 6 but I do feel a little soreness in my legs and sides which is good I think cuz that means I've worked my tsrget areas. Pretty good would recommend. I'll be back if I see results (and if I remember)

On day 10 of the free version and loving the exercises! I love how they are short and doable, with a video of the exercise, countdowns and rest stops. I especially love the "Hooray" sound when I finish the workout. it's something I think I'll definitely with and no ads or annoying continuous subscriber requests, just subtle and gentle which is wonderful.

My sister convinced me to give this a try and I am blown away! In the free version I have access to a 10 minute daily workout that is personalized to me based on the quiz I took when I first opened the app. I can't do any of the other workout routines outside of that but that is more than enough for me and because it's only 10 minutes it feels doable and easy for me to keep up with each day. The music is pleasant and the exercises have been very effective for me! Highly recommend!

honestly I have nothing bad to say.. there are so many workouts it's crazy and they range from doing them in bed in a chair ect. from beginner to a little more experience. I normally dont rate on apps, but I had to take out a few minutes to type this. I have been using for three days now might not sound like much.. but I become so bored with something almost instantly. this has not disappointed worth getting.. signed mom of five!

I just downloaded it so I have not explored it all. but I just did 2 exercises and they made me sweat slightly and they have certain exercises for certain days which I assume its gonna get harder by the day. which is a good idea in my opinion. so far its been pretty good. no complaints accept for the premium. I dont get why they have it but whatever. good app over all, I really recommend it.

Fantastic app, good exercises. I enjoy the music but it cuts out of the "instructor" is not talking. oddly enjoy the little encouragement from the instructor too. The face yoga is a plus. The bite sized workouts are perfect for a busy schedule, obviously you can do more if you have time. Customer support has also been great so far.

the app has a free trial of 7days, I took now abonnement that I pay every 3 months, it has video with counting clock, while you watch the video, the voice explain what to do and how to it and when to begin and also when the pause is. this way you understand exactly what to do so you won't do it wrong, you can put notification and allow alarm as reminder. the app is simple to use. I highly recommend it.it has no adds and video is clean and the voice is loud and,clear and understandable.

I have only just started using the app but it seems excellent. There's lots of information about how to do each exercise properly and they are all timed for you with demo videos so all you have to do is get started! Some of the comments say that it isn't free, but it is if you press the little white x in the top left corner. Highly recommend - for a free app it's really good.

I've been using this app for the past 10 days and it's extremely easy to use + increases my competitive spirit with the streaks and X-day challenges. I love that I can add manual exercises in as well with the number of calories. The exercises are also good and increase in length as time goes by, but the detailed instructions are great at helping you through. I would have loved having daily challenges/missions too or levels. That would make me more excited to get surprise challenges for points.
nice and consistent workouts as well as streching and sweaty exercises

the app is just too good. the workout goal is customised according to your requirements and the app is gamified in a way that it motivates you to log in the next day. it also provides the calories spent and allows you to mix and match different workout styles so that you never get bored. this app is possibly the best one out there. 💞

ONE YEAR LATER: After moving & holiday's I got off track but I kept in the back of my mind that the app was always waiting for me to pick it back up. I have been off & on but this app seriously helps me know I CAN DO THIS! I am trying pilates for the very first time. I have been using this app for a week now, and I seriously love it! Works great and the reminder helps keep me on track!

There is a free version and it's really great and easy to follow. It's the best I've seen so far. I'll probably go premium once I'm a bit more stable financially However, when registering, it looks like they only have the paid version and they offer a 7 day trial. I wish they made the conditions for the 7 day trial very clear so people know they will be charged if they accept it. Tbh, most of the bad reviews are from clients upset about this and it was their reviews that made me aware of it.

I love this app! I've been struggling with right sided SI joint/hip pain with activity which had been super frustrating. after doing this workout for almost 3 weeks, I've been nearly pain free and have even been running with no pain or discomfort! my strength and endurance are improving and my joints seem to move much better. thanks for this great app!!

I subscribed to this app at the beginning of the year when it was on offer. It is one of the best things I've done. There are different levels of workouts and I try and do a routine a day. I have done Pilates in the past and find this app compliments my other keep fit activities such as running and aerobics.

23 y/o Female 5"2 180 lbs, I want to do better for myself. Exercises are beginner-friendly. Some workouts are harder than others, and I'm only on my 2nd day, but I FEEL the burn😫. Visuals help with guiding your body, since the "detailed" explanations are still pretty vague. Wish the model was customizable with diff skin tones and sizes. To watch the model slowly lose weight as you are would be SOO cool and motivating🥹It's still a good app. Doable yoga and pilates w/breaks, ~5-10 mins is great!

this app helped me a great deal . it offers tailored workouts for your body and even considers your injuries . I have never found an app that has helped me this much to boost my confidence as well as regulate my moods throughout. thank you pilates workout, you changed my life ... for the better .

I really wanted a program for pilates for someone who is completely out of shape and has never done pilates. I had several surgeries a couple of years ago, followed by a significant weight gain, and then a significant weight loss, so I wanted it to be a gradual process. This one checked off all the boxes and it's great! It only takes a few minutes a day, and they show you exactly how to do each exercise.

For £30 a year it's identical to a book I already own. I thought it would include music and a voice explaining how to do the exercises. I have memory problems so am to and fro from my phone checking every 15 seconds. After one day I want to cancel it but can't work out how. 0Just buy a book, it's far cheaper and you have it for life!

Hello, I made a payment of ₹3950 through Google Pay on 19th March. I believed I was signing up for a smaller recurring payment and did not realize it was a full upfront charge. I used the app briefly, but stopped as soon as I understood the pricing and realised money was debited from my bank acc. The pricing was not clearly communicated, and this was an unintended purchase. I am requesting a refund within 2–3 days of the transaction. Kindly process this request.

im on day 3, love the free plans. music, and custom exercises. easy to use and demonstrates how to do the exercises well. I'll continue onto 30days and update this review once completed (if I remember lol) but so far so good. you just need to get past all the "upgrade your plan" things at the start.

I thought I was signing up for a free 7 day trial, but a £28 payment was immediately taken. I wish I'd read all the reviews (not just the positive ones on the summary page) before downloading as they show that this is a common complaint and there's no chance of getting my money back. The company should be banned and the app removed. from the store. Total scam.

I've been taken back to the beginning as I got a new phone but I luv ❤️ this app so much that I don't mind. I am a beginner + i use the free version of this + i would definitely recommend this app to anyone. I can do the exercises at home 🏡 + choose which ones I want to do + they feel like they've been professionally tailored for me. I would definitely recommend the face yoga aswell. Seriously, I can't believe the difference since I've been doing them - how much the wrinkles have gone 🫡
