What an incredibly dumb app. I can't even set the measurements right for biking. For example I biked 22.8 km in 40 minutes but it won't allow me to change the speed or the calories. I deleted this stupid app.

Virtuagym is a great product, hands down the best fitness and diet app. However, their customer service is atrocious! They do not respond to requests for service, they ignore email messages and telephone calls. I can't recommend this product because their customer service is so bad!

It has great variety of exercises... Easy to use... Allows to select exercise based on muscle you want to train... Has lots of graphs to understand performance... This has always been an go to app for me whenever i need to loose weight... Love it...

This signs me out quicker than my banking app does. No biometrics to log in so most of the time using this app, which is to get a QR code to get in the gym, is typing my password in. Please just keep me logged in like 99% of other apps. Everything else works fine from what I can tell

Poor system reset password. I had reset password several times. I had double check word by word entered password and conformation charater. It ended " You exceeded the maximum allowed number of login attempts. In addition to your username and password you now also have to enter the confirm code from the image you see below." Firstly, I though it was wrong password. Wasted for few hours trying to solve login problem.

I highly recommend this app and the Workout Jumpies gym in Oklahoma City. Its a gym full of trainers. The owner is always there and the trainers are very reliable and will give you a kickass workout. When you're not with your trainer, this app has a robot man that will show you exactly what to do. It's an easy app to use.

it took several tries to get accustomed to it for my needs. all the exercises and equipment are there, putting them in some organized order was a challenge. looking forward to improve my body composition. thank you for your unique programs.
easy to use with a great design and set up. love this App! has quality videos of exercises if you dont know how to perform something and you can add notes if needed on exercises you perform

I have a plan made whit a PT. I am not able to enter it. The AI is use less and don't care what I told him. Most of the Infos are old and far behind of the actual knowledge about mass grow, fat lose and over all fitness. I have the food tracking app too, both a waste of money and I did the 12 Month pro what was a big waste.

I use this app along with my trainer who logs my workouts/gives me workouts. I'm so happy with it! I added the nutrition app and it sync to the gym app. Easy to use, very informative. Lords of tips. I'm doing really well. I have more confidence in being successful and reaching my goal.
Best app for health freaks. It tracks almost all values. Can be paired with neo devices (smartband and others). Can be connected to Google Fit. In my opinion, it is a must have app.

This app is really good. I installed this app in June and started doing the exercises and nearly after one month I saw some success and continued using this app. The voice guide and tips are helpful. You can also choose your preferred language if you want. There are ads but we can skip them after some seconds. And the app keeps a record of your workouts and also your height, weight, waistline etc. Also there are other different types of workouts you want like pain relief or full body stretch.
app SalingJaga sangat membantu, ahli waris jadi mudah klaim. insyaallah

I first downloaded this app in 2023 and loved it then. I had a surgery and the holidays add some quick weight to my waist and I'm back in the routine again. I love how these easy yet effective at home, no equipment workouts are. The examples, tips and increased difficulty as you go are great. I love that there is a finished button on the rep counts. I don't feel rushed and can really focus on my form when needed. I will recommend this app to anyone looking to workout at home!

This is a great workout for any age or fitness level. I like the fact that when the reps start, I'm the one counting and pushing the button upon completion. This allows me to slow the pace to maximize the exercise. Even a senior like me finds these very doable and challenging.

overall good workouts to do at home..Have been using this app on &off from several years.I would be better if there is inclusion of safety precautions with respect to general medical conditions with each exercise.
this is so good. I love this app really much. Also, it's not that hard and not that easy it's actually so cool, and it's worth they are no ads. You can do this for free
it's literally very nice app for fitness, as it free of cost . it provides many exercise according to you body . ⚡I'm beggner now so let's start together and see the results. best of luck 👍
I have to reopened the app sometimes 10 times because ads pop up and I can't x them.
this app is so good has a variety of workouts you can do for every part of your body love love this!

I love this app, I can exercise whenever and wherever I want. The workouts are not very long (15-20 min.) and you can choose beginner, intermediate and advanced. There are 20-30 second breaks between each exercise but you are allowed to skip the break if you don't need that much time to get into the next position. I'm noticing some slimming at my midsection which is my problem area. I really love this app.

Amazing app! I don't have a single complaint! Ads are obviously not pleasant, but they need to be there to actually keep the app running. I absolutely LOVE that they don't pop up in the middle of a workout!! They wait until you are finished. There are 3 levels. Beginner, intermediate, and advanced. This is a free 3 month program right here!! And you can even make your own workouts!! I love this app and definitely recommend it!!!!

This app is awesome. I started with beginners, some places I do more then asked and then hit next. Sometimes I skip the breaks, I'm glad it has this buttons, sometimes I don't need 30sec break. My endurance has gotten better within a week. Every day it's a different set of exercise which I love because it's not routine. The app is not to strenuous that makes you want to quit. I Feel like it's the right amount and level to keep one motivated!

Great app for a beginner like me! Tracks weight and waist size. Has "skip" buttons in case a particular exercise can't be done, and the coaching tips offer ways to make exercises easier or harder depending on your needs. Also has exercises for beginner, intermediate, or advanced workouts; though I've only used beginner so far and have lost several pounds and inches! Android LG Stylo 4

There are so many free workouts for more than I'd ever think of. I'm working my way through them. It's only been a month but there are crazy positive results. Usually I don't take days off but this gives multiple rest days a week on beginner. Super beginner friendly. If I can keep up this habbit up for 5 more months I'll need to upgrade. Based off what science says it takes to engrain a new behavior into everyday life. I hope I get there. 100% recomend this app. It's become one of my favorites.

Easy to get into exercises that are just fine for even folks like me who have never been to a fitness studio. This is the first time I actually managed to stick to a workout routine, in large part due to it being so short, each session taking between 15 and 20 minutes if you stick to the 30 second break time. The app interface is easy to get used to and shows all information you'd like to see. Adds are of course there, but they are not obtrusive and easy to close. Would definitely recommend

Best app I've found! I have been using this app and eating healthier for a little over a month now, and I've already lost 10 pounds. You can select the intensity of your workout, it gives you breaks when you need them, and there is a computer voice moving you along through each exercise. Highly recommend this app. Definitely not disappointed in my results.

I enjoy the programs it has, and it goes up at a gradual pace. I'm not doing the days as designated, but after 80+ workouts, I have noticed results and have gone down a pants size. Don't think of this as a quick fix, but more of a lifestyle incorporation along with a good diet. The app is neat and also allows you to build your own workout routines or go for preprogrammed ones. I do recommend it, but always consult with your doctor if you have preexisting conditions.

Very good app! I love how it includes rest days, and how you can sync with Google Fit. I feel like I personally can stay more committed with this app because of the schedule it provides, whereas others you just kind of have to figure it out yourself, and as a beginnerlike myself that can get confusing. I also really enjoy how the exercises aren't the exact same every day so you dont have to worry about getting bored or losing interest! Also no equipment needed! Yay!

I love it! This is only my 3rd day using this app and it is so much better than any other app I've used! It doesn't give you exercise to over work yourself and it makes it fun so you don't even notice that you're finished with it... seriously love it! Thank you! P.S. the only thing is that I could not figure out how to change the time on the "morning work out alarm" ..it's set to 8 am and that's not the time I wake up, but there's doesn't seem to be any other options for those .. so I don't ever use the alarms :(

Love this app. I've been doing yoga, walking more and substituting snacks with fruit. Combined with this app, I've lost 10 lbs in a month. I put on weight during lockdown but I don't go to the gym or like running. This was a great app to keep me on track. The sessions don't take too long so I squeeze them in before work, on lunch or after work. I really like the extra tips given while you're doing the exercise. The little ping after every exercise makes me smile. Will keep at it!

Im not the type of person to leave reviews. I was looking for a fitness app after having a breakdown about my belly fat. I thought the app looked cheap however i wanted to give it a try. I am so thankful! It keeps you motivated and keep tracks of your workouts. I love how they show each move with animated drawings aswell. Really helpful. I actually look forward to working out everyday now. Great app yall.

Great app! I love how I can make my own routines (work out/stretching etc) the kcals should be fixed because I feel like 20 minutes of moderate work outs wouldn't burn much calories. I like the automated voice telling you how long is left of the current activity. I feel like 30 seconds is too long of a wait between the different workout activities. Maybe the user could set their own break times inbetween. Another thing I live is the tracking. I bought myself a scale so I can put in my weight every couple days and so far the graph is on a down slope! Keep up the good work every one!

Great so far! I'm only on day 3, but with this app, I'm more motivated to excercise because it gives you small workouts to start and buils up to bigger ones as you lose more fat. So I'm not dreading having to so a big workout. It also has 30 second breaks between each excercise so you can catch your breath, or you can skip the break if you're ready to go to the next excercise. I also like that it has a BMI calculator, and you can see how you're improving when you change the input.

Actually free, ads aren't annoying, instructor is easy to follow! There is a bell/ light whistle blow to tell you when to start and stop with her counting down. 1 ad is at the beginning and end of a session plus when you start the app. It's low cost to remove them if you choose. I don't find them intrusive, and I'm usually annoyed by them! I had this app on my old phone, and I found it gave me a routine with a workout between 8-20 minutes I could easily follow. I lost about 8 kg then!

I have been using the app for a few weeks now and am really enjoying it! Most surprisingly, I am sticking to it!! The difficulty level is spot on and suggestions during the exercise to make it easier or harder are helpful. Everything that I've tried is achievable so I even go on "discover" to do extra exercises!! Anyone who knows me would be surprised. Recommended!

It's a good app. Lot of good abs, legs, cardio, and stretches. It has options to choose workout routines based on levels or customize on your own. The duration/repetitions can also be customized. It doesn't have all the work outs obviously. I wish I could type in or add pix of workouts that the app doesn't have. Also it would be nice to able to search workouts instead of scrolling up and down.

This app is really encouraging in terms of beginning a routine and tracking your progress. (among other tracking options). The reminders are great too! The only reason I give it 3 stars is because of the ads that pollute the app, literally on every single page / step of the workout. It puts you off big time. I'd rather pay a few quid to remove the ads and enjoy the app for the qualities it has. There didn't appear to be an option to turn them off either. Otherwise, I'd have given 5 stars.

This app has four different levels. At the end of each workout, it asks: too easy, just right or too hard. The app adjusts or switches the workout that is giving you issues with another that works on the same area. It has adequate rest days. It tracks your water intake, waistline, weight, etc. The only bad thing about this app is that it keeps sending reminders: "It's been 3 days..". Like I don't want to be consistent, but the app keeps motivating me. Try it, over and over again 😅😅

Love the app. I can do a 1-minute plank now! It's a little weird that it keeps telling me to follow the voice prompts to pace my exercises correctly, yet there doesn't seem to be anything that actually does that. As far as I can tell, I have all the options turned on, and I can hear all the tips during the exercises. But nothing that keeps me from going too fast as the tip suggests. One thing you need to change. During the side leg lift exercise, the voice says, "This exercise will help burn your love handles." That's misleading and not how exercise works. It'll tone your obliques, but you can't target fat in specific areas.

I love this app. It gradually builds you up to more intense workouts. It always incorporates stretching. And provides news and tips to a healthier you. For me, to pay, i think it was $2.99, to enjoy the app with no ads was a win. You can still enjoy the app without paying by watching an ad but its all worth it. I get a good sweat going after every workout (i like to do 2or3 as a session).

This is the second app from Leap Fitness group that I am using and I can say that I have really benefitted from them. There are some features that I miss in this one though like "announcing the next exercise". This feature is currently missing in some of the routines. also I don't get any background music unlike in the "Female fitness app". Overall, I really like the app and I say keep up the good work.

Awesome app. Simply the best out there. However to the development team, it'll be nice if you add a profile area. I just switched phones and I lost all my work out history, how sad. I was sure that there was a profile area, only to discover that there wasn't , please add one so it backs up data that can be restored upon change of devices. Thank you.

This app has been amazing up until the latest update. Calorie tracking has become more difficult, the search function is extremely slow. Calories have also stopped tracking correctly, a 350kcal activity now shows as 57kcals. Unsure if this is isolated or other people are experiencing the same issues. Would love to see a fix for this soon.

Very good app! Not too many pop up adds. You only see them when you finish a workout and then maybe a few else where. My favorite part about this app is that you can customize your own work outs! I really enjoy this because I can choose which exercises I want to use and exactly how many of them I want to do. Now I can create workouts that fit me and my time schedule! Oh plus it's more fun to make your own!!! 🙂🙃😁

Cannot recommend this app enough. I was apprehensive at first to use an app for a fitness routine, but now I can't imagine doing it without it! It takes all the worry about trying to set up a routine on your own, especially if you don't know where to start. The daily routine takes ~20 min to do, less if you skip through the 30 sec breaks. The best thing about the app is it has all the features you'd want (i.e. timer, progress tracker, custom routines, exercise guides) included within the app.

The main workout takes about 15 minutes, optional morning/evening stretches about 5 minutes. I can record/monitor my weight and waistline, including numbers from before I started the program - you can fix the numbers if you wrote the wrong thing down. The voice of the coach is a robotic, but that doesn't bother me. I'm a beginner - the workouts are challenging enough to feel it, but not so hard that I'm in excessive pain; also short enough that I can squeeze it into my busy life. So far so good.

I love this app so much, but it gets 3 stars for disrupting anything I do on my phone (work, etc.) by reminding me to drink water every 15 minutes. I drink 2 liters of water a day based on my weight, I do not need to be reminded. They should have an option to turn it off and it's odd that they dont. Please fix this and the app would be 5 stars perfect.

I really do like this app. The excersize isn't hard, and it comes in multiple levels of difficulty based on how advanced you are at working out. Within those levels you can choose standard or easy on certain excersizes. It has helped me to start working out every day and lose a couple inches on my waist so far. The best part is there is rest days, so you are being consistent and you don't feel guilty not working out on those days.

I saw the review saying pop up ads were popping up outside the app, and I tried it anyway because the dev's denial sounded so convincing that they wouldn't do that. The app is great btw, I got a great belly burn even in the short workouts...but alas, the pop ups occur. Come out of lock screen, get off a call, etc. If you can deal with that, get this app. If not, well...

If ur wiling to do the work it's a useful app. I started with the beginningers course to ease back into working out but mostly cuz I do it before bed. It's great for those who are just starting to work out, need to get back into a routine or just want a light workout. The app gives detailed instructions and even more detailed videos. With bonus workouts to download. The workouts aren't very long either, and u can go at ur own speed. I'm looking forward to trying the higher levels.

I very much enjoy this app, the way it does my thinking for me. The exercises seem well matched for my abilities, which it is helping to grow. Four stars, not five, because the robot coach voice is really annoying; it sounds super cheap. I wouldn't think it would be that difficult to get someone to record the instructions and encouragements for a better exercise experience.

I am extremely out of shape and let my weight and health get away from me. This app is amazing, yes it requires you to want to use ut but it us set up great for beginners and even experienced. Is very user friendly and i love the tips it guves while you are working out. For example, it can say "you should not feel tension in your chest and shoulders" during a specific workout and I will realize I do so I adjust to do correctly. I highly recommend and may try some if the apps by them as well.

I LOVE this app - it has been my personal trailer during covid-19 lockdown! Different exercise routines for each day, and rest days incorporated. You can select between three levels depending on your current fitness level and work your way through. I've recommended this to countless people so far and has, in my experience, been much more beneficial than a gym membership without having to spend a penny.

This is the only workout app that has worked for me. It has really made a difference and motivated me to focus on fitness. Simple and minimalist. Neutral concise instructions. No irritating encouragement and motivation, no upbeat music, nothing cutesy. I also appreciate the option to turn off the coach's voice and just have the timer sounds. There are ads on the free version but no distracting features. Worth paying for it however.

Updated review (STILL 5 stars): If I could write more than one 5 star review I would. I have been using this app for 3 months. I HAVE ABS NOW. I'm toned. I never worked out this long before. Best part, I can do it right at home. With the update there is no shortage of workouts to try with all areas of the body and varying intensity. Program allows for gradual steady build up. Also, you can add resistance bands, dumbbells and leg weights to increase difficulty/muscle. I love it!

I've never stuck to an exercise regimen as consistently as I have to this one. The challenges are perfect for each level, and the results are astounding. My lower abs are my problem area, and these exercises have made a noticeable difference. I'm currently on intermediate level. I look forward to doing the advanced level exercises when I'm ready for them.

Simple to use, intelligent, clear. Just found out you can change not only the voice, but the language too, and you can adjust how long you want to rest between exercises, whether you want to hear useful tips or not...plus there are videos linked to each exercise if you want to see the movement in more details....All that for free! We are a spoilt generation!

Thank you for fixing the below issue. "After the latest update, only 'percentage change' is showing in 'Widget display'. It can't be changed to 'Price change' view like before. Price change is very much needed. Also auto refresh is not working in 'widget'. Thank you."

app is great. the workouts allow you to progress without too much stress, but the in app coach gives you tips and modifications for if the exercise it too difficult or too easy, as well as the option to add more rest time between them if you need it. i have only two gripes: you have to stay in the app for it to continue going through the exercises, and sometimes, when i update my weight (bcs the app works and im losing) itll say my previous weight is still the lowest, not my current weight :/

This is the best ab app ever. The site is user friendly and offers several kinds of exercises. The workouts show how to do the exercises so you don't have to look them up. The calendar marks the workouts for you. You can also set alarms for reminders. Yes you have to watch an ad to download some of the exercises but the ads are short. It also does not ask for any personal information. I was able to sync my devices just by using my Google account.

I feel great and motivated. After 2 weeks people say they can see a difference and can see a 6 pack coming in. They ask what I have been doing diferent. You can tell the progress being made through the program. as you do them more, the easier it gets for you to do the exercise. It takes a lot of mental strength. I'd recommend listening to music while you do it. I found it helps keeping you more likely to follow through the program entirely.

Honestly, this is simple and easy. You are able to choose your level of fitness and it caters to you. It tracks all your progress on a calender and you can add in your weight and waist line if you are into that. The best part is that the app is that it also gives you rest days. It does not discourage you, instead it encourages me personally because if I miss a few days I can either pick up from where I left off or start from the beginning! No judgements!

Sleep tracking has become inaccurate over the last 3 months. It always doesn't correctly log the go to sleep time & it used to do this accurately before. We can't edit the auto-tracked sleep timing data without deleting all the tracking data & adding sleep timing data without any tracking data. Sometimes the watch doesn't catch my sleep or wake times properly & I should be able to edit that data. This is such a simple feature that has been missing for years.

after using the app for nearly 2 years I've come to the conclusion that the app is not accurate. There are many occasions there are gaps in the data, inferring that I'm neither asleep or awake. There are many occasions when I've woken up, yet according to my watch I've asleep, so overall I wouldn't recommend. first time I've been disappointed with a Samsung product.

Response below does not jive with app. Nothing called "Pedometer". Went to "Steps" but could not find options in menus to stop and restart counting. Tried force stop and clearing cache. Still not working. The app changed recently after months of daily use as a step tracker. This week, instead of tracking steps in real time as I walk, it now counts in the background and updates the displayed steps count roughly every 2 minutes. I have been unable to find a fix for this. Frustrating!

Need to work on playing nicer with other apps. I use a different app for food tracking. I did try this one first and didn't like it, didn't find it to be very user friendly. But this app is very limited it what information it will share with other health and fitness apps. It will only share daily steps. If this is the best samsung can do for a health and fitness app for their watches and trackers I won't be wasting my money on them again.

This is my go to app for tracking my calories intake, menstrual cycle and excercise times. It's generally good but since the last update has been a bit laggy when searching for items to upload in calorie counter. Edit: good grief. You made the app fine again and now you've changed it again. It's time consuming enough counting calories without having all of your buttons moved around on the screen, making you have to stop and figure out where everything is. Stop fixing what isn't broken!!

it's decent, particularly when integrated with a Samsung watch. two annoying things: the Strava connection has to be reactivated frequently, and there's no way to set an interval less than 5 minutes on the workout routine which is dumb. if I'm doing a rest between intervals, I should be able to make that less than 5 minutes long.

Please fix the bugs in the latest update! This would've been a 4 * review until the latest update. I've gone from calorie & macro counting everything I ate every day for the past 10 months ( 5 stone+!) to not being able to use the food tracker AT ALL! I've got a Samsung 24 ultra so there really is no excuse. the app crashes constantly as soon as you click on the search button to type in your food item. then IF you get through the search is even worse than before! rest of the app is good

The sleep is so buggy it's unbelievable. God forbid you wake up in the middle of the night briefly, it only records the remainder of the sleep in the app, even when the watch shows both! No amount of syncing fixes it. This data is then carried over to other apps and messes up stats everywhere. Really poor. And from what I can find this has been an issue for a long time so hell knows what these people are actually doing at their end to improve the app and service, sounds like little.

Worked great for the first year, but some time over the last few months it stopped sleep tracking entirely. I have to manually log the hours I was asleep (it even gives me the exact times! so it knows but refuses to log it?) and hasn't shown sleep stages for ages. I have done a restart, checked all permissions, everything suggested online, and it's still useless. Would have been nice to know before I blew so much money on an overpriced watch.

I dont know what they were thinking with this last update. The app is very slow and clunky now. when I search for meals, it will just show me the list of ingredients, not the actual name. Everything was divided up into multiple menus for no reason. If I want to update a meal or check what's in it, it takes me to the name change section, so I end up typing instead of scrolling. The app takes about 5 full seconds to open the search bar after I tap it as opposed to being instant like before.
sejauh ini asuransi syariah yang preminya paling manusiawi dan ui nya paling bagus

The app lacks a sort of sophistication other fitness apps have. Cartoon animals and color coded amoeba? Nah. Oura's interface is something to envy; made for adults, not children. Next issue is with sleep tracking. No, it didn't take me 3 hours to fall asleep last night. I spent some time reading. I should be able to make this adjustment without deleting the whole sleep record. I can't. Third, data trends. Not prominent or easy to get to. Shouldn't have to port data to excel to make sense of it.

You have destroyed the main reason I bought the watch by removing temperature readings. +/-° is completely useless information. It's meaningless to cycle tracking, it's meaningless to sleep. Who is this for?? Fix it! There are people that rely on that information! You could have at least given us the option to choose how to display it instead of forcing +/-° Show me my temperature chart readings, this is insane!

The app and its connectivity to the ring is good. Decent features but some serious limitations: 1. Only 3 workout types can be added to main screen 2. You can't add workouts after you've done them. So if you forget to turn them on your phone before you start, its lost. This is a big issue and makes the Ring less reliable than others to track all activity properly. 3. Main screen not very customizable.

This app occasionally misses huge amounts of steps. For example, I walked for an hour in the morning and it only counted 300 steps while my Google Fit counted 5000; I have my settings on accordingly to count all steps and NOT pause. The global leaderboard is also a scam because people clearly use step increaser gadgets to get over a million steps over a couple of days. It's also impossible to leave the service; an error page always shows up and I've cleared my cookies. Impossible to leave.

I appreciate this app for all of the activity tracking and stats provided, as well as the calorie counting. That said, they've majorly dropped the ball with not having a simple deficit calculator. what's the point in tracking activity calories automatically if users then have to MANUALLY calculate what their daily deficit is? This is something you NEED to be able to see progressing through the day (like steps) in order to stay on track. Incredibly frustrating bc it's SO SIMPLE.

Something is severely wrong with this app's sleep detection. Shortly after the last update, the sleep data accuracy suddenly went drastically downhill. It used to be pretty accurate, but the last several nights, the app thinks I'm awake for 3 plus hours at night, but I know for a fact I was sleeping. I used to get sleep scores of 80 to 90, but the last few nights, my sleep score has been going down to 20 - 50s. This morning it says my sleep ended at 5:30 am when I actually woke up at 8:30. Why?

This is still the best app, but still subpar. My app updated this morning and the recent sluggish search was resolved. The titles of my meals are not displayed the new search, which is annoying. They still need to update the micro-nutrients for foods (hasn't been for 5 years). Looks like some of the detailed tracking disappeared for running without a watch such as air time and impact, but I don't use that as much.

It used to be more accurate than it is now. The sleep tracker, in particular, had features that are no longer available, where you would take a closer look at times and sleep cycles. It also used to be very accurate, but it will now completely miss entire several hour chunks of sleep.

The app won't launch without a sim card. This is absurd but I found an asinine workaround. You just need literally any sim, even an inactive or invalid one, the first time it opens. Luckily I had an old micro sim in a box of junk. That worked. I'm guessing buying a sub-$1 sim on YoHo would work. Still absolutely nonsense that this is a requirement. Fundamentally not a free app if you need to purchase a sim to use it. Samsung's response to my initial review is borderline insulting.

Not loving the recent update. In particular - logging meals. I have to keep tapping the search symbol multiple times before it loads and it looks clunky and is difficult to use. My mood check in doesn't automatically remind me anymore and I can't figure out how to set that back up. No issues with other sections so far.

I'm happy with most of the features, especially the challenges and walking together. It's a great motivation. But the daily activity tracking is completely broken for me. The past three days are completely blank and say I haven't taken a single step, or burned a single calorie, even though I've walked more than 10,000 steps each day and even tracked walking activities through the app.

app seems pretty useful, I don't like that I can't enter an exercise after I already did it. I completed a 10k step walk (mostly uphill or stairs!) and couldn't enter my path or data afterward. It counted the steps but didn't count it as exercise? idk if im misusing something, but it's really frustrating. next time, I'll make sure to enter it in before so It counts. I probably won't use this app much longer though.

Comments in 2023 are still relevant. Trying to simply calculate my steps while walking. Of course it doesn't work. I can choose the exercise, the date, what I want to track, but there is nothing to start the app monitoring my steps. No "Start" no "Go"....nothing. Did it record my activity? No. It's still worthless Weak, food tracking doesn't work, customization doesnt work, no pathway to get support unless you give them a bad rating on Google Play, then a phone number miraculously appears.

Well, everything works, but the experience is full of rough edges. Issues in particular that annoy me daily; there's a painfully long delay to search for a food in the calorie tracker, which appears to be getting worse the longer I use it. Also, medication compliance forgets all history whenever the schedule is changed.

It's mostly accurate, but it regularly misses the mark. one day, I did around 10,300 steps and burned 534 calories, but 2 days later, I did around 10,500 steps but somehow only burned 320 calories. That doesn't really make sense to me. I'm walking the same route, at the same speed, on the same days. It definitely needs to be fixed. Otherwise, this app is pretty decent

Do yourself a favor and go with Garmin! Do not use this app!! The app isn't good at recording a run or following a GPS signal. I've frequently found that when viewing the run on Strava it's got me running 5 minute miles and it cuts off the distance. Now the app just doesn't get GPS at all despite saying it's connected. I've just recorded a run it stated this finish time and yet nowhere to be found absolutely ridiculous. I can't rate any lower than one star otherwise I would.

The app itself is good, tracks well, functional, organized. My only question really is, why is it whenever my "Energy Score" is high, my sleep score seems to be below average or average and oddly enough, vice versa (low energy score = high sleep score.) Although the energy score gives a more accurate sense of what I'm feeling for the day, it's still strange to see conflicting data. Update: My issue has been resolved. 😊

I haven't had my fit 3 for long. at first, the app worked great, and then less than a month in the app was dropping steps compaired to the watch. I did complain to samsung. A week later, I was able to do a band update. This solved all the syncing issues. Although it does lose sync easily if i'm in another room. So far, it hasn't lost steps or information. It does make it tad slower catching up after. it's all good. I love both. The app is very informative and encouraging 👍

This rating is subject to change. For now, though, 2 stars. There are several reasons for this, but the main reason is the running coach. it doesn't care what my performance has been on "the test", what answers I gave on the survey, what Samsung workouts can automatically record and track. It reccomended I complete a 10K in 1h 8 minutes. I can easily get a 10K done in 55 minutes on a bad day. Today it took a while for my HR to lower but app tells me to reduce rest days. Dangerously inaccur

Started using Samsung Health years ago for tracking medications and steps. Now, I use nearly everything from blood glucose to sleep. It could use better instructions, but after using it for awhile it's easier. Health Connect is great for syncing other apps. One feature I discovered is syncing my health records from my doctors. It makes a searchable database that us easier to use than the Mayo Clinic app. My wish would be a web interface to enter and export when on a PC.

Update: I gave 2 stars before. They responded to my last review & I called them. Now, there was another update, and it's worse because it only gives me an average of my oxygen level rather than the range. Their customer service is excellent, which is why I upped the stars, but they keep making changes with updates that make the app useless for me. It was great until the updates.

I used to really love how the calorie counter would work. This new update is not it. I like being able to go back and see all the foods that I've eaten throughout the week. And now I can't even see what I've eaten on that day unless I'm looking at, you know, the first page. I tend to eat a lot of the same food, so I like being able to go and see what I've eaten throughout the week, but now I have to look up the food every time I eat it and then search for It.

UPDATE:The dev response was useless. I'm talking about adding a new feature that can help this app. ORIGINAL: Samsung Health needs an ability to extract workouts in .fit and or .tcx formats. Garmin does it. and u can sync up your workouts with other devices programs etc. but samsung doesn't and I can't use it going further. I'm using version of 6.29.5.017.

Too fond of bling. From time to time they jazz up the interface so that instead of just displaying how many steps it spends a long time counting up to the number. I walk 25000 steps a day and have my routes divided to segments that I like to record time and steps for. Usually I am within a few seconds and steps, and within 50 steps over 25000. Having to wait while the app enjoys counting is not cute. It is just annoying. Very annoying. It might take 10 seconds to show the number.

Overall, I like the app. Food tracking isn't too difficult. Weight tracking syncs up to my scale app perfectly. I wish it could connect to my FitBit because I wear that all the time. I wish it had a selection for exercises like yoga, pilates, and barre in the exercise menu. Edit 7/9/25: The step tracking has gotten terrible. The app used to sync with my Google Fit, which connects to my FitBit so I could keep accurate track of my steps. Now it doesn't, and I am constantly short on steps

app is ok, but for a few months I've had a problem with my sleep recording. I wear the watch every night to sleep and a few months ago (idk exactly when) the app stopped showing me my sleep record and a breakdown of my sleep, and only shows time, and some days it doesn't even show the record at all. I've searched on Google and tried every troubleshooting possible and just cant fix it. it was my main reason of using this app and now I cant even use that.

App is killing my battery now with latest upgrade to One UI 7. Its using the majority of my battery even in deep sleep. please fix. I love the step challenges but might have to switch to Google Fit or another tracking app that won't kill my phone battery. I literally have to keep my phone on the charger now or I won't have enough battery when I get off work and I am not even using my phone.

Got myself a Samsung watch, mainly to count steps for a walking challenge I'm doing. However, it would count steps whilst driving, so none of the figures I have been logging are accurate. No option to adjust sensitivity or edit step count on the app and not sure that I can trust any of the other readings it offers, so essentially it was a useless purchase and two weeks later I'm taking the watch back and have deleted my Samsung health account. Expected more from Samsung!

This recent update made the sleep app less helpful. I'd wake up everyday looking forward to the results but recent changes implemented in updates make it no longer helpful for me. Why change something that was working well and provide less info. I've relied on the watch to make sleep interesting but now it's missing information and the app scores are lower than before. It totally missed I fell back to sleep this morning. Very disappointed. Please bring back the old ver.
Used the app on a slow network. It took a few seconds longer to load, but all features worked fine.

I've been using the app for more than 3 years now, and it's just great! LOADS of useful features. I especially enjoy global challenges, badges you get for finishing, so rewarding! My suggestions for devs: 🥑 Would love to see more vitamins and nutrients in the food tab 🥑 💤 Sometimes in the middle of the night I wake up for 3 mins, but the app counts it a separate sleep record. It doesn't contribute to my sleep score anymore... 💤 ❤️ HRM on app-started workouts randomly works or not, why? ❤️

Recently upgraded my Samsung Watch to a Watch6 Classic. I really want to use the snoring detection but I just can't get it to work. App notifies me multiple times a day to open it "at least once" to detect snoring even though I open it on my phone and watch several times a day. Every night no snoring detected either because of an app update or phone restart "during sleep". Mic permission is on. Phone is charging (wirelessly). Yes - all the settings are correct.

10 years later, it still does not provide simple features like adding additional walking steps in order to fix it manually. An Intermitting fasting timer would be amazing. Step Sensitivity is needed (low, medium, high) step length, gender. Stride length calculation. I hoped for improvements in this area that still did not happen😥 👎 I like this app, but I expected a bit more. Since launch, this app has not added much need improvements or new features 🤔 🙄

I wish logging meals was easier. The food database is quite limited compared to other apps and it makes it difficult to log meals quickly and accurately. There has got to be a way to make it easier to log meals throughout the day so it isn't nearly as cumbersome. It would be nice if there was a meal logging widget, also that included the barcode scanner option.

Pitiful. Since recent updates it is impossible to have the reports we once had. Now, they are only approximations. For heart as an example you no longer can see minute to minute if it is going up or down no matter how you try to change it in the settings, making the readings worthless. Sleep is the same way, the graphs are useless. And look at weight... no way to follow a weight loss program because the display is ridiculous. The worst health app ever. Will abandon my Galaxy 7 watch soon...

It used to work great. I only use this app to track my sleep and walks. I used to be able to get my workout details for my walks, but not anymore. Now, when I click on my walks, it goes to the next screen and the loading gif just spins. The details won't come up. I let it go for 10 minutes one time and the details screen never came up. The sleep tracking is somewhat consistent. Every once in a while it misses a night. So now I am on the search for another tracking app for my walks.

I like the app, but when I add information about a walk (manually, because sometimes I don’t take my phone with me on a walk), Steps aren't added automatically, only calories. I think it would be easy to add (i mean, technically, as a new feature). When I add distance and walking time, it should add steps. I think this is important, I track my steps every day, but it’s not always possible to take devices with me

Early versions were excellent. But every "update" has resulted in enshitification to push the product as a service. want to see your workout data? approve syncing your health data with our cloud. want to track your route through gps? agree to our data collection policy. Want to reach out through the app to complain about the issues you are having with the app? you must agree to our data collection terms. This app has taken a sharp downturn in quality and I don't know if I will keep using it.

All this functionality sounds so good. Unfortunately, all it is good for is tracking your steps and checking your heart rate. None of the other data is anywhere close to accurate, and most of it doesn't work correctly in conjunction with a Samsung Galaxy watch which is frankly absurd. Today I tried to use my Samsung Galaxy Watch 5 to track swimming laps. After much fiddling I finally got it to start, but stopped by itself 3:14 later, despite the fact I swam for 50+ min. Sadly, this is the norm.

I appreciate that security is important when it comes to my cards. The recent update, however, has made the use of cards very annoying, so much that I'll probably start using my physical cards. 3 out of four times I need to reconfirm with fingerprint, it's slow, inconsistent and often times I end up spending extra 30 seconds to make a small payment. It's far from clear when I need to reconfirm and when not.

Handy enough app. Able to see any loyalty cards I have and movie tickets. Only problem is some times it acts up when paying. A common message is I've moved the phone too fast when I haven't, so I've to close the app and try again. Other times it'll be fine then up pops "verify it's you" so I've to do that then pay, little annoying. Other times it doesn't do anything, though this may be an issue with the card machine than the app.

A year later update: the carousel method to choose a card hasn't been improved at all. It's still much slower than previously to go through cards as you need to wait for the animation to complete each time. If you frequently use different cards (I travel a lot), then it's very frustrating. It used to be so much better with just a simple list of cards on the app. At least the reliability got better.

Fine for payment cards - does everything I want and expect. Rubbish for other cards. You can't assign a name to a loyalty card, so you can't manage severally the same type. You can't add a card which isn't already in their list. Until my other loyalty card app was killed off I thought this app was fine, but the loyalty card section is not fit for purpose.

I haven't made the move completely from card and cash to using this app, but as concert and train tickets are able to be saved here, I expect my use will increase. It's definitely handy to have, though I do like having a backup in case anything else goes wrong! Shame I can't add things like my library card to it, that's the one I forget the most!

Must have toolkit for any day trader wanting just the right amount of technicals in the drill down charts. Automatic Earnings calendar alert for the tickers I am tracking without me having to specify, daily Put/ Call chart. YF is THE tool I go to every morning

Please allow sort by name. Please add share loyalty card. Would be useful in family scenario. Also search button would be useful at the bottom. Add Payment card gets in the way of you cards. There is already Add to Wallet in the bottom right. Have filter like loyalty cards, memberships etc. The GUI could fit more cards in the screen size. Like Stocard and Klarna.

my user experience leaves me very frustrated 98% of the time. I'm considering going back to tapping a physical card. I consistently have to either verify a few times with thumb print, even when I've just used it , or the phone "moved too fast" even when it didn't or it literally does nothing at all, and I'm stood at the checkout, waiting & trying & nothing is happening at all... even when I come out of my wallet & refresh , it still does nothing... it's so intermittent. it's not good enough !!

The app really went down regarding quality of the service. For months it has been declining my transactions, every single one! Unlocking pin code did not help! I even changed my card completely and it still cannot connect the card. Removed and installed the app, did not work. The app clearly has become absolutely unusable! No point of me downloading it again..!

It's good but desperately would love the ability to customise the loyalty card boxes (custom colours and images) like you can do on Samsung's version. I also have a lot of loyalty cards and scrolling to find the one I need is frustrating. We need a search option and the ability to change how they are shown, from the current list to a grid etc.

The interface for managing items is horrible. It would help if they can be grouped eg. Loyalty cards, payment cards, train tickets, flights. And a simple way to archive or delete items e.g by swiping them left or right, or even getting a context menu would be great rather than clicking into each one, scrolling down and clicking another button.

Having to repeatedly "verify it's you" makes it unusable. The need for verification shows up intermittently and unpredictably, so you try to pay, fail, and then realise oops, this time I have to verify. Also, to verify takes 2 steps - you have to tap on the top of the screen to show the fingerprint scanner, so you have to stop and use two hands. Make verification always mandatory above a user-set threshold and just auto-display the fingerprint scanner at the bottom without the extra tap. Easy.

The concept is good, execution is okay, but there's some terrible features. Automatically adds new things to the top of the list rather than ordering passes/tickets by date, and the UI reordering function mustn't have had much use testing, you have to move them bit by bit and tend to accidentally blast other ones out of order while trying to shift one down. If drag-reorder tool is too hard to implement properly - doubtful, other apps do it fine - then there should be other sorting options.

💸I RECOMMEND Google Wallet! It's a robust way for me to make my in-store, online payments, digital tickets, & various other store transactions in 1 central location. I've never had issues, so you will get security & privacy with Google Wallet. It keeps all your essentials protected. (It will not share your real credit card number, so your payment info stays safe) You can make contactless payments, add any loyalty program cards, & any gift cards. It's a FREE VERY USEFUL android exclusive app!💸

Works, but works TOO well. sometimes if i have my phone out just a few feet away from a payment thing (like to grab coupons/check my balance), it opens the app automatically, without my permission, and tries to pay. how the hell is that secure? no "confirm payment!" button or anything, just. phone open? payment terminal 1 foot away? PAY PAY PAY PAY PAY. i have to disable NFC just to keep it from accidentally opening (and theres no quick "disable/enable nfc" button). does NOT feel secure.

With the introduction of the verify is you function, and I no longer have fingerprint on my phone as had to replace the screen, wallet is a waste of time. I may as well use the physical care and do chip and pin. I get the security perspective, but phones are generally secure, maybe the verify kicks in less often?

I have passes loaded and used to be able to add tickets from Ticketmaster. It wasn't ever seamless. After speaking with a person where the tickets are purchased I was told that he have the biggest problem because it doesn't work well to Google Wallet. I was told to you guys a web browser instead to open up the tickets and save them to the wallet. Hopefully this solves the issue.

The new double authentication is so frustrating and unnecessary. It should be a personal choice whether you want this feature or not. Please give the option to turn it off. It is causing delays at shops. *Edit yes, we know its for extra security but it is flawed, unnecessary and a real nuisance.

Used to work flawlessly on my old phone but now I've bought a new phone and it now wants my pin before tapping on every transaction. Also, I can no longer make purchases in apps anymore because after verifying myself, the google pay app gets stuck on the black loading screen. How can you mess up something that worked perfectly before?

After 10 Years of use, acceptance is improved but maybe 5% of businesses have never adopted it. ..still gotta carry cards around. Not clear when I need to confirm my authorization to use or not. having my phone unlocked is just not enough sometimes. no clue if it's gonna work from the lock screen.

I tried to add the warranty card for my car. Instructions *claim* that I can add images that don't have barcodes, but it's not true. Good app for instant loans with smooth performance and fast response time.

I was able to use this app until about 3-4 months ago. Now I get nothing but a black screen when I login. The app appears to be open. I've checked for app updates, I've uninstalled and reinstalled, and each time I get nothing but a black screen. Essentially, the app is 100% unusable for me. I gave it two stars because when it worked, it worked well.

The latest update is not an improvement. It has turned a really good easy to use, user friendly, app into a frustrating user experience. It was much better when all my loyalty cards appeared in a single list, allowing me to simply scroll up and down to find the one I want. Accessing some in favourites, and the rest separately is not progress! Purchased train tickets always remained at the top of the list until I cleared them. Now i have train tickets disappear before I exit the platform barrier.

Great for payments, not too handy with store cards. At the moment, the membership number for some store cards is way too small. If the barcode doesn't scan, it's difficult for cashiers to read the number. Longer card numbers are even worse. There should be an easy method of making the numbers larger (e.g. double click to zoom in/enlarge). Whilst many UIs are improving accessibility, this app is quite poor. It's not alone - my previous store card app's replacement is equally poor.

My gift cards are Gpay listed, but paying with GPay often doesn't work. Also, I have noticed a big issue, that the app doesn't work when you don't have mobile data switched on or mobile coverage, unlike other wallet apps. Another nuisance is that it requires password authentication just to use your loyalty cards,not only creditcards

Used for years now to pay for most things. A little annoyed by the constant need to verify my identity in the wallet even though my phone is secured properly. Small price to pay for some additional security I suppose. Lately I seem to be getting the flickering screen bug in the wallet app. Hope this is addressed soon, since this is also getting irritating.

Overall, the app is good. It's easy to add cards and find your loyalty cards. There are no issues on usability. The only thing I wish could be changed is the ability to sort through your cards vertically rather than horizontally. Sometimes, it takes a while to swipe to the card I want to use. Instead, move the loyalty cards to the left/right swipe and allow your payment cards to be scrolled through vertically.

I wasn't having this problem before until now. I have been trying to add new CC numbers to the wallet. Contacted the bank and they saw no problem on their side. Contacted Google Wallet, they don't see any issues. I'm frustrated because this was so easy to use until they updated it. I have two different credit card companies and they both keep showing that they are having a hard time verifying the cards and to call the bank. Which I already did. I can't use this app as easily as I did before. Grr

I dont like the new layout of my cards at all it was better before i nicely organised them in alphabetical order. now when im stood at the tills in shops i have to root around and takes a lot more clicking on things to find the relivent loyalty card which is last thing u need at the tills. go back to old layout of loyalty cards but with an option to organise them into either most used at the top or in alphabetical order which i prefer. thanks

I've only been using Google Wallet with just one credit card a few months here in U.S. and works very well. Don't understand how its rated 4.4 of 5 when half the reviews are only 1 or 3 stars.

update 03.19. I'm downgrading this to one star. I've completed all of the troubleshooting steps listed below and I still have to hit the payment button multiple times for a payment to go through. It's a great App, however when you have several lookalike cards, and have nicknames for them, it would make more sense to have them displayed on each card, than having to tap on each one of them, which more annoyingly in itself in that it springs back to the default card & you have to remember your place in the sequence of cards and swipe along to try the next. People have busy lives & don't have time to remember card numbers. I don't understand the idea to not display nicknames on cards?

The app was great until a few weeks ago when it deleted all my id cards without any notification and will not let me add them back just keeps saying try again later. App. no longer working. Several previously loaded IDs have disappeared and cannot be reapplied. worked great but I went to check on a card today and all my non payment cards have been removed. driver's license, loyalty cards, insurance, etc, all gone. it appears that anything that was just a photo only, and not a barcode, got removed and the option to readd them is gone.

On top of that, re-authentication often leads to transactions being aborted so I have to call for someone to reset the transaction which is very annoying for me as well as the personnel at the counter.

Overall, as a payment card app, it works mostly well. A few quirks at times, but no major issues. Lots of complaints about how it handles gift cars and rewards cards, though. The interface basically sucks, especially if you have many entries. Sorting is missing, organization is terrible, as is lack of grouping. View is not optimized with excessively large tiles, etc. No separation of gift and loyalty cards. The limited web access and insufficient tie to Google accounts is still a problem, too.

Google wallet is broken. 50% of the time when I open the app, the fingerprint scanner screen slides in from the right on a loop, rendering the app unusable. Restarting my phone is the only solution. There is a bug in this app.. when you make a contactless payment, Google reports an amount to your messages that may not match the actual billed amount. This is because it may "report" an amount not including a tip. The tip may go on after the contactless "ding" happens. The credit card company gets the correct amount but your message from Wallet does not.

App glitching, start it about a couple weeks ago. when you first click on it it's flickering and shaking super fast, and therefore unusable. so far I've been able to close it, clear some of my RAM with the built-in optimizer and usually that will allow it to work. but if I have not used it and a couple hours it'll start back.

4/18/2026: Very convenient, except as of late it flickers when opening the app and crashes. It takes a few times of opening and closing before it's stable and will take my fingerprint. Highly recommend Google Wallet because it keeps all your essentials in one secure place — payments, tickets, loyalty cards, and gift cards. I have been very happy with this app. I will be bummed if it ceases to exist. I use it in conjunction with the website, which was changed in 2025 (making it less easy edit). However, no better portfolio trackingy method found. Pitty that publishers change great app/websites just to "update" it.

Beats a lot of other apps in looking up info on various stockmarket tickers. Useful app for following your stocks but the calculations of your portfolio gains really suck. Great app that i use daily to give a quick view of what's happening with my and my wife's share portfolios, which I have stored within the app. The earnings feature, which tracks our dividends based on our portfolios, is also useful.

Easy to navigate the site. Loads of information. Always available without any glitch. I am a big fan of Yahoo finance site. Simply perfect. The possibility to create multiple portfolios using csv files and the quick glance on the titles in your holdings (including pop ups alarm) is really what you need and expect by an app for this purpose

For a free app this one is pretty good. Data is delayed slightly but the offering of data points is decent. I am using Google pay from 2-3 years my experience with this app is very nice. But suddenly from 2 days I'm having issues with the opening of mobile recharge section and electricity bill section. I want to pay my bills but it is not opening don't no why please solve this issue

A message "something went wrong" is being displayed everytime I make a payment and the tick sign showing a transaction as successful doesn't come. However the transactions are successful anyway. Kindly help.

Using Google Pay as a student feels effortless and smooth. The interface is simple, payments are instant, and it saves a lot of time during daily transactions. I especially like how secure and reliable it feels. Perfect for recharges, splitting bills, and small payments. Overall, a very user-friendly experience.

For the past 15-20 days I've been facing issues with the Google pay. While making payments get technical error but payments were done successfully money debited credited messages were received but still getting technical error. Kindly check the issue. Testing needs to be done properly before production release. technically you need to check all the different mobile versions .

I have selected to use the Google pay app rather than using other apps. This app needs additional support of the automatic locking system once the transaction is over and the app is closed. This app opens automatically when I open it next time without asking for any password or enabling biometric fingerprint. Whether I'm right or wrong. please check.

The app performs really well and even includes Splitwise-style features. However, the issue is that it doesn’t have a “settled up” option. Because of this, if someone hasn’t paid yet and still adds a split, you can’t ignore or exclude it. If this enhancement goes live, I will be much more happy as it's my only go to app while performing any transactions. I will change my ratings to 5.

PhonePe is very good and smooth to use. I have faced almost zero payment failures, which is great. However, after making a payment or checking balance, when I press “OK” or “Done” to return to the home screen, a pop-up ad appears. I understand ads are needed since the app is free, and I have no issue with that. But these pop-up ads are frustrating and add an unnecessary extra step. It would be much better if they did not interrupt the flow after every action.

Great Experience. Just a suggestion for improvement [since there is no specific email ID to suggest improvement] The place below wherein we enter the amt for payment has a symbol, which opens up various graphics usually depicting the reason for payments like "Movies", "Groceries", etc. There is a calendar symbol which displays the current month's name and names of past 6 months. I suggest that in addition it should display the names of upcoming 6 months as well, which would be very helpful.

I've been using PhonePe for a while, and it's been a smooth experience. The app is simple, fast, and reliable for daily payments. Transactions happen instantly and the interface is easy to use. It offers many features like bill payment, recharges, and money transfers all in one place. Overall,very convenient and secure—definitely a 5-star app!

Very bad experience. The customer support is very bad, with only automated responses to work with.

​I am writing to express my extreme frustration regarding a serious issue with my PhonePe wallet. ​Despite having a balance in my wallet account, the app is incorrectly displaying zero balance. Customer service is the worst. Phone pe suddenly decided to unlink my phone number, which made it not possible to receive payments. Very disappointing experience with PhonePe. The app works fine until there is a problem. Once you get stuck, there is no proper way to speak to customer support or get the email. I’ve been using PhonePe for over six years now, and it’s easily the most reliable UPI app I’ve tried. Transactions are lightning-fast, and the UI is incredibly clean and easy to navigate. It has made everything from daily grocery payments to monthly electricity bills so much simpler. Highly recommended!

Misleading and frustrating experience. UPI registration itself keeps failing with no proper error messages, and there is no real customer care to help. There is no clear explanation for the difference. Be very careful before trusting this app with your money.

I had a very bad experience with PhonePe transaction issue. On 1st April, I transferred ₹8000 to another UPI ID. My PhonePe account has been blocked for seven days. I haven't received any help from anyone. After speaking with customer service once, the option to contact them is no longer available. I have 8 years of good working experience. However, this month I faced a hacking incident and money was stolen. I did not receive any refund, and the complaint process is not working. I have been using PhonePe for a long time and it is one of the best UPI apps in India. The app is smooth, fast, and very reliable for daily payments and money transfers. Absolutely worst experience, I am trying to pay my bill that PhonePe fetched for me and I am unable to do it because PhonePe Protect is activated which is blocking my transaction. The app is working flawlessly. app doesn't work anymore. if you try to go to credit, it reloads for about 5 minutes and then says unavailable. this has been going on for weeks. PayPal is seriously one of the most convenient apps for managing money online. it gets the job done. 👍 only thing, it's real easy to add money via bank (5 to 10 business days, when you meant to use your debt card (instant). This app is a good one, in a lot of ways.
